  https://www.businessinsider.com/inside-rolls-royce-factory-where-six-figure-cars-are-made-2026-7

## Research linkage

- automotive paint complexity and field variance;
- ASTM E2194/E2539 multi-angle measurement;
- AxF and measured-material architecture;
- OEM coating-process literature;
- pigment manufacturer catalogues;
- hyperspectral vehicle-paint data;
- metamerism and polarization sources already retained in V10.

---

# CP11-PRACTICAL-ORDER — How this changes implementation

## Before choosing a target shader

1. choose a real OEM finish class;
2. locate official name and model/year/market;
3. capture current official option evidence;
4. locate code/order data if available;
5. classify layer/process;
6. record UI swatch only as proxy;
7. collect official configurator renders;
8. collect diverse real photographs;
9. seek measured/repair data;
10. state missing information.

## Before exposing artist controls

Ask which OEM population supports the control.

Examples:

- `Frozen Grade` is supported by real matte option families;
- `Coachline Mask` is supported by two-tone and hand-line programmes;
- `Colourstream Travel` is supported by disclosed interference options;
- `Mirror Polish` is supported by high-end finishing disclosures;
- `Process Mottle` is supported by application variability;
- `Object Match` is supported by bespoke programmes.

## Before declaring realism

The shader should be compared against:

- at least one official standard finish;
- one premium metallic;
- one pearl/multicoat;
- one matte;
- one bespoke effect.

This prevents overfitting the architecture to one exotic show paint.

---

# Legacy V11 active-manifest snapshot

The following manifest described V11 when it was current. It is retained for append-only provenance. The active one-file manifest is CP12 at the end of this document.

# CP11-ACTIVE-MANIFEST

```json
{
  "title": "Car Paint Research Compendium v11",
  "version": "11.0",
  "date": "2026-07-15",
  "research_cutoff": "2026-07-15",
  "authoritative_artifact_count": 1,
  "authoritative_artifact": "Car_Paint_Master_Source_v11_FINAL_UNIFIED.md",
  "project_blender_baseline": "5.0.1",
  "forward_reference": "5.2 LTS",
  "content_policy": "append-only; no digest or index replaces the complete corpus",
  "new_v11_domain": "OEM paint intelligence, factory options, codes, configurator proxies, bespoke methods and Blender mappings",
  "critical_rule": "No physically meaningful universal paint hex is claimed for effect or layered finishes.",
  "dynamic_data_rule": "Factory options are captured by model, model year, market, date and source snapshot.",
  "next_phase": "Populate the OEM registry alongside Blender laboratory experiments; use official options to select representative paint targets."
}
```

# V11 conclusion

OEM colour catalogues are one of the most valuable bridges between the literature and production.

They tell us:

- which physical finish classes manufacturers actually sell;
- which effects are mainstream or exceptional;
- which processes command premium positioning;
- how paint is tied to brand, model, era and body design;
- which shader modes deserve practical implementation;
- where UI colour, repair identity and physical appearance diverge.

The registry must remain dynamic and source-qualified. It must not become a fabricated list of “official hex codes.”

V11 turns OEM paint data into:

- a target-selection system;
- a metadata standard;
- a configurator archive;
- a historical palette;
- a process database;
- a Blender preset taxonomy;
- an experimental input population.

# V12 implementation, node-connectivity and reference-integrity audit

## CP12-VERDICT — Was V11 referenced properly?

Not completely.

V11 contained a very large and valuable source base, but its citation quality was inconsistent.

### V11 local audit

- 293 URL occurrences;
- 163 unique URLs;
- no consistent canonical reference-ID system;
- many early source cards with strong metadata;
- many later entries represented only by a raw URL;
- dynamic OEM and configurator pages without a universal snapshot format;
- some paywalled standards cited by identifier but not clearly marked inaccessible;
- commercial/community sources not always distinguished visually from primary physics sources;
- two stale Blender manual paths;
- legacy version sections capable of surfacing superseded interpretations during retrieval.

A raw URL is evidence that a source exists. It is not a complete scholarly or technical reference.

V12 does not delete the raw source history. It adds:

- canonical core-reference IDs;
- source roles;
- authors/publishers;
- title;
- year/version;
- DOI or stable URL;
- access status;
- support scope;
- limitations;
- claim linkage;
- a complete retained-URL inventory;
- an active supersession map.

## Reference completeness levels

### R0 — Mention

A name or unsupported statement.

### R1 — Locator

A URL, DOI or standard identifier.

### R2 — Identified reference

Title, author/publisher and year/version.

### R3 — Scoped reference

Identified reference plus the exact claim it supports and its limitations.

### R4 — Audited reference

Primary content opened or source code located during the current audit.

### R5 — Reproduced evidence

The referenced behavior was independently tested in Blender or measurement.

V12's key foundation references reach R3–R4. No Blender implementation reaches R5 until the project laboratory runs.

---

# CP12-STATUS-LABELS — Node and implementation evidence

Every active node card uses one or more of these labels:

- `DOC-V`: behavior verified in official Blender 5.0 documentation;
- `SRC-V`: relevant implementation file located in Blender source;
- `PAPER-V`: primary research content opened or metadata verified;
- `STD-V`: current standard/specification content opened;
- `COMM`: community or developer observation;
- `INFERENCE`: direct technical consequence;
- `HYP`: proposed project architecture;
- `LIVE-5.0.1`: executed in Blender 5.0.1;
- `OBS`: visually observed in project renders;
- `MOTION-V`: validated in animation;
- `PROD`: accepted for a declared production scope.

At publication of V12:

- many cards are `DOC-V`, `SRC-V`, `PAPER-V`, `STD-V`, `INFERENCE` or `HYP`;
- none should be read as `LIVE-5.0.1` unless later evidence is appended.

---

# CP12-SUPERSESSION — Active interpretation map

Append-only preservation creates a retrieval hazard: an older, plausible statement may still appear before its correction.

This map defines current active interpretations.

| Topic | Legacy interpretation retained in history | Active V12 interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| projected-pixel LOD | `feature_size × focal_pixels / depth` presented as broadly sufficient | valid perspective approximation only after correct camera, units, resolution and sensor handling; exact surface footprint also depends on orientation and projection |
| focal pixels | width × lens / sensor width | prefer `0.5 × effective_pixels / tan(FOV/2)` using Blender camera `angle_x/angle_y`; handle orthographic cameras separately |
| tangent frame | use Tangent output and cross product directly | re-orthogonalize against the chosen normal and provide a degeneracy fallback |
| flake tilt | arbitrary shaped random angle | use a declared distribution: uniform solid-angle cone, Gaussian slope, measured distribution or fitted mixture |
| coat-effect attenuation | add manual attenuation | Principled/OpenPBR coat is the default vertical layer and already attenuates substrate; manual correction is disabled unless controlled tests prove a residual mismatch |
| AO | convenient material-specific shading factor | Cycles-only AO node, expensive and not a replacement for light transport; EEVEE requires baked/GN alternatives; never multiply blindly into albedo |
| Bump Filter Width | possible general normal filtering solution | useful pixel-width control for Bump derivatives; not LEAN, LEADR or general NDF filtering |
| Light Path | portable ray semantics | Cycles ray semantics; EEVEE exposes approximations and aliases several outputs |
| UI swatch | initial physical paint colour | official display proxy only |
| closures/bundles/repeat zones | available architecture | candidate architecture until live Blender 5.0.1 verification |
| V9/V10/V11 manifests | current package identity | historical provenance only; CP12 manifest is active |

---

# CP12-INFORMATION — What stock shader nodes can and cannot know

This is one of the most important practical connections in the entire corpus.

## Directly available or representable in stock shader nodes

| Information | Typical source | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| surface position/coordinates | Texture Coordinate, Geometry, attributes | coordinate space and physical scale must be explicit |
| shading normal | Geometry/Normal paths | may include interpolation and normal modification |
| true/geometric normal | Geometry True Normal where supported | diagnostic comparison is essential |
| tangent | Tangent node | UV/radial/axis basis; re-orthogonalize for custom vector math |
| view/incoming direction | Geometry Incoming, Camera Data | not the direct-light vector |
| camera depth/distance | Camera Data | lacks render size and full projected footprint |
| UV maps | UV Map / named UV | stable but seam- and texel-density-dependent |
| object/instance random | Object Info | one value per object/instance, not per flake |
| stored geometry fields | named attributes | GN/script preprocessing can supply missing geometry information |
| ray category/depth | Light Path | engine-specific semantics |
| texture filtering | Image Texture/internal nodes | not arbitrary user-controlled convolution |
| Bump derivative sampling | Bump Filter Width | internal nearby samples, not a general derivative API |
| material parameters | node values, drivers, custom properties | best route for camera/render metadata supplied from Python |

## Not generally exposed to stock material nodes

| Missing information | Consequence | Alternative |
|---|---|---|
| current direct-light vector per light | cannot manually evaluate a complete light/view half-vector sparkle model | let the BSDF respond; OSL/custom renderer; controlled proxy only |
| direct-light radiance and solid angle | cannot compute universal source-aware sparkle probability | explicit BSDF/microgeometry; per-shot preprocessing; OSL/custom code |
| exact pixel footprint/ray differentials | distance-based LOD is approximate | OSL derivatives, baked moment maps, Python metadata, renderer filtering |
| arbitrary neighbouring shader samples | no generic procedural blur/convolution | image preprocessing, GN Blur Attribute, OSL multisampling |
| full surface statistics | no panel-wide mean/variance from one shading point | GN, bake, Python |
| wavelength spectrum | RGB approximation only | external spectral fitting/LUT/reference renderer |
| polarization state | ordinary nodes cannot evaluate Mueller/Stokes transport | external capture/model or specialised renderer |
| prior-frame history | material cannot classify TAA/denoiser history | compositor/render pipeline analysis |
| proprietary OEM formula | shader cannot infer it from a colour chip | measured/reference fitting and explicit uncertainty |

## Governing invention question

When a node graph seems impossible, ask:

1. Which information is missing?
2. Is it fundamentally unavailable, or merely not exposed in the shader?
3. Can Geometry Nodes, Python, OSL, a bake, a texture, a driver or an explicit proxy calculate it upstream?
4. Which approximation preserves the most important information for the shot?

---

# CP12-OWNERSHIP — Parameter and lobe ownership

A recurring failure in large procedural materials is hidden double control.

| Phenomenon | Sole primary owner | May modulate | Must not silently control |
|---|---|---|---|
| body colour/opacity | `CP_Groundcoat` / finite-layer branch | substrate visibility | coat reflection |
| mottle | `CP_MottleField` | local body/effect concentration | coat normal |
| grain | `CP_GrainField` | diffuse-scale effect variation | discrete sparkle |
| flake presence/size | `CP_FlakeField` | event density/coverage | coat roughness |
| flake orientation | `CP_FlakeOrientation` | effect response | panel geometry normal |
| angular travel | `CP_AngularTravel` | effect colour/energy | camera post |
| coat roughness/haze | `CP_Clearcoat` | buried-effect visibility through physical layer | base albedo |
| orange peel | `CP_CoatTopography` | coat normal only by default | base/effect normal |
| AO/cavity visibility | `CP_AORouting` | declared lobe weights | texture/albedo destruction |
| scratches/swirl | `CP_SurfaceCondition` | coat normal/roughness by defect depth | fresh base colour unless paint is breached |
| chips | `CP_Damage` | layer exposure | intact-coat roughness everywhere |
| camera bloom/glare | camera/compositor | displayed highlight footprint | material energy |
| LOD | `CP_EffectLOD` | representation weights | paint identity |
| quality tier | `CP_RenderState` | implementation cost/detail | physical family parameters |

Every accepted group must state which table row it owns.

---

# CP12-NODE-CARDS — Exact active implementation specifications

## CP12-NODE-001 — `CP_COORDINATE_STATE_B50`

**Status:** `DOC-V`, `SRC-V`, `HYP`  
**Purpose:** Produce stable physically interpretable coating coordinates shared across separate body panels.

### Inputs

- rig-space position;
- optional effect UV;
- optional condition UV;
- metres-per-Blender-unit;
- panel ID;
- coordinate mode.

### Outputs

- `P_METRES`;
- `P_MILLIMETRES`;
- `UV_EFFECT`;
- `UV_CONDITION`;
- `PANEL_ID`;
- `COORD_DIAGNOSTIC`.

### Preferred sources

1. Geometry Nodes stores a shared rig-space position attribute.
2. Texture Coordinate `Object` using one dedicated Empty shared by all panels.
3. named UV map for authored effects.

### Wiring logic

```text
P_source
  → Vector Math SCALE by metres_per_Blender_unit
  → P_METRES
  → Vector Math SCALE by 1000
  → P_MILLIMETRES
```

### Prohibited defaults

- Generated coordinates for final physical paint across differently bounded objects;
- Camera, Window or Reflection coordinates for attached paint fields;
- implicit object scale.

### Diagnostics

- XYZ emission;
- checker at known millimetre pitch;
- panel-boundary continuity;
- object-transform test.

### Failure signatures

- panels have different flake sizes;
- pattern resets at object bounds;
- field swims with camera;
- scale changes when transforms are unapplied.

### References

`REF-BL-SRC-CAMERA`, `REF-OPENPBR-001`.

---

## CP12-NODE-002 — `CP_CELL_SEED_B50`

**Status:** `DOC-V`, `HYP`  
**Purpose:** Generate stable cell identity and local coordinates without exposing raw Voronoi cells as visible flakes.

### Inputs

- `P_MILLIMETRES`;
- nominal cell pitch in millimetres;
- Voronoi randomness;
- seed offset.

### Construction

```text
P_CELL = P_MILLIMETRES / cell_pitch_mm
P_CELL → Voronoi 3D
```

Use outputs conceptually as:

- feature-point Position → cell feature position;
- Color → stable cell seed vector;
- Distance / Distance to Edge → optional local masks.

A local coordinate may be formed from:

```text
LOCAL = P_CELL - FEATURE_POSITION
```

Exact socket availability must be inspected in Blender 5.0.1 for the chosen Voronoi mode.

### Random-channel rule

Generate separate deterministic random channels from the stable cell seed.

Do not reuse one random output for:

- presence;
- diameter;
- azimuth;
- tilt;
- efficiency.

Correlated channels create visible procedural causality.

### Output roles

- `CELL_ID_VECTOR`;
- `LOCAL_COORD`;
- `RAND_PRESENCE`;
- `RAND_SIZE`;
- `RAND_AZIMUTH`;
- `RAND_TILT`;
- `RAND_EFFICIENCY`.

### Failure signatures

- reptile scales;
- visible cell borders;
- one cell equals one obvious hexagonal flake;
- ID changes under camera motion;
- random fields correlate visibly.

---

## CP12-NODE-003 — `CP_TANGENT_FRAME_B50`

**Status:** `SRC-V`, `PAPER-V`, `HYP`  
**Purpose:** Create an orthonormal local panel frame for flake orientation, scratches and process direction.

### Inputs

- chosen base/panel normal `N_RAW`;
- Tangent output `T_RAW`;
- fallback axis.

### Active construction

```text
N = normalize(N_RAW)
T_PROJECTED = T_RAW - N × dot(N, T_RAW)
T_LENGTH = length(T_PROJECTED)
T = normalize(T_PROJECTED)
B = normalize(cross(N, T))
T = normalize(cross(B, N))
```

The final recomputation reduces accumulated non-orthogonality.

### Degenerate fallback

If `T_LENGTH < epsilon`, choose a fallback axis least parallel to `N` and form:

```text
T = normalize(cross(FALLBACK_AXIS, N))
B = normalize(cross(N, T))
```

A stock-node implementation can use Absolute, Compare and Mix nodes to select a fallback. It must be tested on all panel orientations.

### Handedness warning

For imported tangent-space normal maps, use Blender's Normal Map node rather than treating encoded RGB as arbitrary vectors. The custom frame is intended for project-generated fields.

### Outputs

- `N_FRAME`;
- `T_FRAME`;
- `B_FRAME`;
- `ORTHOGONALITY_ERROR`;
- `DEGENERATE_MASK`.

### Diagnostics

```text
abs(dot(N,T))
abs(dot(N,B))
abs(dot(T,B))
abs(length(N)-1)
abs(length(T)-1)
abs(length(B)-1)
```

These should remain near zero within floating-point tolerance.

### References

`REF-BL-SRC-TANGENT`, `REF-CG-NDFF-2016`.

---

## CP12-NODE-004 — `CP_FLAKE_ORIENTATION_B50`

**Status:** `PAPER-V`, `HYP`  
**Purpose:** Generate declared, testable distributions of flake normals.

### Mode A — Uniform solid-angle cone

Given independent uniform random values `u` and `v`:

```text
phi = 2πu
cos_theta = 1 - v × (1 - cos(theta_max))
sin_theta = sqrt(max(0, 1 - cos_theta²))
D = cos(phi)T + sin(phi)B
N_FLAKE = normalize(cos_theta N + sin_theta D)
```

This is uniform over the cone's solid angle. It is not necessarily a realistic automotive orientation distribution.

### Mode B — Gaussian slope distribution

Using independent uniform `u1`, `u2`:

```text
r = sqrt(-2 ln(max(u1, epsilon)))
phi = 2πu2
sx = sigma_x × r × cos(phi)
sy = sigma_y × r × sin(phi)
N_FLAKE = normalize(N + sx T + sy B)
```

This directly models a bivariate Gaussian slope prior and supports anisotropy.

### Mode C — Measured/fitted mixture

A mixture may represent:

- tightly aligned platelets;
- a broader secondary population;
- process-direction bias;
- repair or edge zones.

### Separate controls

- orientation spread;
- anisotropy;
- azimuth bias;
- presence;
- area;
- morphology/lobe width;
- local reflector efficiency.

Do not collapse them into one “flake strength.”

### Diagnostics

- encoded normal;
- tilt angle;
- azimuth;
- slope X/Y;
- distribution histogram from a bake;
- mean and covariance.

### Failure signatures

- invalid black vectors;
- grazing normals pointing below the surface;
- strength changes without angular redistribution;
- visible global/world alignment;
- distributions changing with object rotation.

---

## CP12-NODE-005 — `CP_PROJECTED_PIXEL_LOD_B50`

**Status:** `DOC-V`, `PAPER-V`, `INFERENCE`, `HYP`  
**Purpose:** Blend discrete and homogeneous effect representations according to apparent feature size.

### Perspective approximation

For horizontal and vertical axes:

```text
effective_px_x = resolution_x × resolution_percentage / 100
effective_px_y = resolution_y × resolution_percentage / 100

focal_px_x = 0.5 × effective_px_x / tan(angle_x / 2)
focal_px_y = 0.5 × effective_px_y / tan(angle_y / 2)

diameter_px_x ≈ diameter_world × focal_px_x / max(view_z_depth, epsilon)
diameter_px_y ≈ diameter_world × focal_px_y / max(view_z_depth, epsilon)
```

Use one declared scalar:

- maximum;
- geometric mean;
- direction-aware projected size.

### Orthographic camera

A perspective formula is invalid.

A basic horizontal estimate is:

```text
diameter_px_x ≈ diameter_world × effective_px_x / ortho_width_world
```

The exact mapping depends on aspect and Blender's orthographic framing.

### Important omissions

This scalar approximation does not account fully for:

- feature orientation;
- surface foreshortening;
- local curvature;
- lens distortion;
- depth of field;
- camera aperture;
- ray differentials.

### Blender architecture

Camera Data provides depth but not all required render/camera metadata.

Supply precomputed values through:

- driven Value nodes;
- material custom properties;
- node-group sockets;
- Python update handler;
- OSL derivatives for the research branch.

### LOD weights

Use overlapping smooth transitions:

```text
W_DISCRETE + W_TRANSITION + W_HOMOGENEOUS = 1
```

Never use a hard threshold.

### Identity-preserving convergence

As features become subpixel:

- discrete event count should decrease;
- mean energy should remain plausible;
- normal variance should become roughness/NDF broadening;
- body colour and angular family should not change;
- no pop should occur.

### References

`REF-CG-LEAN`, `REF-CG-NDFF-2016`, `REF-OSL-001`.

---

## CP12-NODE-006 — `CP_COAT_TOPOGRAPHY_B50`

**Status:** `DOC-V`, `STD-V`, `HYP`  
**Purpose:** Give the outer coat independent multiband surface topography.

### Inputs

- short-wave height;
- medium-wave height;
- long-wave height;
- amplitude per band;
- physical scale per band;
- anisotropy/process direction;
- Bump Filter Width;
- coat base normal.

### Construction

```text
H = A_short H_short + A_medium H_medium + A_long H_long
H → one final Bump conversion
Bump Normal input = coat base normal
Bump output → Principled Coat Normal
```

Combining related scalar heights before normal conversion is the default.

### Bump Filter Width

Official Blender 5.0 behavior:

- measured in pixels;
- default `0.1`;
- intended to enable subpixel filtering for stable results;
- larger values can create bevel-like filtering for step textures.

This is a renderer/node sampling control, not a physical wavelength or a replacement for NDF moments.

### Units warning

Bump `Distance` is an effective shading multiplier. Do not claim a direct measured micrometre amplitude without calibration. True physical height comparisons may require displacement, a measured height map or a verified conversion.

### Output ownership

The default output affects only Coat Normal.

Base/effect normals remain independent unless a documented lower-layer defect requires coupling.

### References

`REF-BL-BUMP`, `REF-MATX-001`, `REF-OPENPBR-001`.

---

## CP12-NODE-007 — `CP_EFFECT_VISIBILITY_CORRECTION_EXP`

**Status:** `STD-V`, `HYP`  
**Default:** disabled.

### Reason

OpenPBR defines coat as a vertical dielectric layer over a substrate. The coat interface and medium already alter energy reaching and returning from the substrate.

A second arbitrary attenuation curve can:

- double-darken the substrate;
- break energy;
- attenuate body colour incorrectly;
- conceal a renderer/material setup problem.

### Required test order

1. Build one fixed effect substrate.
2. Use Principled/OpenPBR coat only.
3. Sweep coat roughness and absorption where available.
4. Compare against controlled references.
5. Enable a correction only for a repeatable residual mismatch.

### Correction scope

Any empirical correction must:

- operate on the separated effect contribution;
- not multiply the coat lobe;
- not multiply the complete material;
- preserve mean body colour unless evidence says otherwise;
- declare whether it approximates transmission, blur, visibility or measurement response.

### References

`REF-OPENPBR-001`, `REF-MATX-001`.

---

## CP12-NODE-008 — `CP_AO_ROUTING_B50`

**Status:** `DOC-V`, `HYP`

### Cycles branch

Official Blender 5.0 AO node:

- is Cycles-only;
- is expensive;
- exposes Color, Distance and Normal;
- exposes Samples, Inside and Only Local;
- becomes invalid with OSL active on the OptiX backend.

Use the scalar AO output as a visibility proxy.

### EEVEE branch

Use:

- baked AO;
- Geometry Nodes/precomputed proximity;
- texture/attribute;
- no fake assumption that the Cycles node is portable.

### Lobe routing

```text
V_BASE   = remap(AO, base_strength)
V_EFFECT = remap(AO, effect_strength)
V_COAT   = remap(AO, coat_strength)
```

Expected ordering for local cavity approximation:

```text
base/effect influence ≥ coat influence
```

The exact relationship is experimental.

### Prohibitions

- AO multiplied into the colour texture destructively;
- AO used to fake all shadows;
- AO treated as dirt;
- AO hidden inside an unlabeled Base Color chain.

### References

`REF-BL-AO`.

---

## CP12-NODE-009 — `CP_LIGHT_PATH_SIMPLIFY_B50`

**Status:** `DOC-V`, `HYP`

### Cycles use

Potentially simplify expensive effect evaluation for:

- deeper glossy rays;
- diffuse rays;
- non-camera secondary paths.

Preserve:

- average energy;
- coat;
- body colour;
- reflection identity.

### EEVEE limitation

Official Blender documentation states that EEVEE has no real ray concept. Several outputs are unsupported and mapped to Is Glossy; Ray Length becomes camera distance.

Therefore an EEVEE branch must be separately designed, not assumed equivalent.

### Test

- direct camera;
- mirror;
- glass;
- neighbouring body panel;
- reflection plane;
- screen-space reflection where relevant.

### References

`REF-BL-LIGHTPATH`.

---

## CP12-NODE-010 — `CP_DIAGNOSTICS_B50`

**Status:** `DOC-V`, `HYP`

### Required AOVs

- true normal;
- shading normal;
- normal deviation;
- coordinate state;
- panel ID;
- cell ID;
- flake presence;
- flake size;
- flake tilt;
- flake orientation;
- mottle;
- grain;
- discrete/homogeneous LOD weights;
- coat height bands;
- coat normal;
- AO base/effect/coat;
- process fields;
- damage/condition masks.

### Vector encoding

For display only:

```text
encoded = 0.5 × vector + 0.5
```

Retain raw numerical data where possible.

### Normal deviation

```text
deviation = 1 - clamp(dot(normalize(N_true), normalize(N_shading)), -1, 1)
```

This is a diagnostic, not a proof that all deviation is wrong.

### AOV naming

```text
CP_N_TRUE
CP_N_SHADE
CP_N_DEV
CP_CELL_ID
CP_FLAKE_TILT
CP_LOD_DISC
CP_LOD_HOM
CP_COAT_H_SHORT
CP_AO_EFFECT
```

The scene manifest must list configured AOVs.

---

# CP12-PRACTICAL-GATES — Build order and acceptance

## Gate 0 — Version and scene integrity

- Blender 5.0.1;
- copied test file;
- applied/recorded scale;
- scene units;
- camera and colour management;
- manifest export.

## Gate 1 — Geometry truth

- mirror material;
- strip lights;
- true/shading-normal AOV;
- topology and modifier ablation.

## Gate 2 — Coordinate truth

- millimetre checker;
- separate panels;
- object transforms;
- animation.

## Gate 3 — Solid paint baseline

- body colour;
- base interface;
- coat;
- no flakes, mottle, dirt or AO.

## Gate 4 — Cell and tangent state

- stable IDs;
- independent random channels;
- orthonormal frame;
- diagnostics.

## Gate 5 — Effect orientation

- distribution bake;
- compact-light response;
- object rotation.

## Gate 6 — Footprint and filtering

- resolution/lens/depth matrix;
- discrete-to-homogeneous convergence;
- no pop.

## Gate 7 — Coat topography

- multiband strip reflection;
- Bump Filter Width sweep;
- DOI/haze separation.

## Gate 8 — Layer interaction

- Principled/OpenPBR coat baseline;
- effect-visibility correction only if proven;
- AO engine branches.

## Gate 9 — OEM target

- official identity;
- finish class;
- UI proxy;
- official renders;
- real photographs;
- missing-data record.

## Gate 10 — Production qualification

- animation;
- reflections;
- engine;
- denoising;
- performance;
- fresh environment;
- asset packaging.

---

# CP12-METRICS — Stop relying only on visual adjectives

## Multi-angle colour

Where measured/reference values exist:

- CIE Lab or an explicitly declared colour space;
- ΔE00 per geometry;
- face/intermediate/flop lightness and chroma;
- illuminant-specific errors.

## Sparkle

Per controlled ROI:

- event count;
- active pixel/area fraction;
- intensity percentiles;
- maximum after unclamped recording;
- event-size distribution;
- nearest-neighbour distribution;
- temporal event persistence;
- source-size response.

## Graininess

Under diffuse illumination:

- luminance RMS;
- band-limited contrast;
- spatial autocorrelation;
- PSD by frequency band.

## Coat/DOI

Using reflected strip/edge:

- 10–90% edge-spread width;
- gradient peak;
- contrast transfer;
- line waviness;
- band-separated displacement.

## Temporal stability

- frame-to-frame absolute difference;
- optical-flow-aligned residual;
- event births/deaths;
- persistent event tracks;
- LOD-weight derivative;
- denoised versus raw comparison.

## Energy

- white-furnace-like controlled environment;
- mean outgoing brightness by roughness;
- coat-on versus coat-off difference;
- custom correction on/off;
- check for clipping and hidden Clamp nodes.

Metrics do not replace judgement. They stop judgement from being the only record.

---

# CP12-TROUBLESHOOTING — Symptom to evidence path

| Symptom | First suspected domain | Inspect | Discriminating action |
|---|---|---|---|
| flakes slide with camera | coordinates | coordinate AOV | replace view/reflection coordinates with rig/UV |
| reptile/hammered appearance | raw Voronoi visibility | cell/local masks | use cells only as identity/seed |
| sparkle boils | filtering/seed | cell ID, LOD, raw animation | freeze seed; reduce frequency; test moment/LOD branch |
| broad softbox produces pinpricks | light-independent fake | unclamped event AOV | remove Emission/threshold mask; let BSDF respond |
| cavities turn dirty black | AO/albedo | AO and base colour AOV | remove albedo multiplication |
| matte coat leaves razor-sharp effect | layer architecture | coat/effect AOV | verify effect is actually beneath coat |
| matte coat becomes too dark | double attenuation | correction weight | disable empirical attenuation |
| grazing black artefacts | vectors/math | normal length/dot AOV | normalize; clamp domains; inspect negative square root/log |
| panel flake scale differs | coordinate scale | millimetre checker | shared rig/GN attribute; apply/record scale |
| object rotation changes flake statistics | world-space error | tangent and normal AOV | use panel-local frame |
| EEVEE reflection differs radically | engine/ray/probe | Light Path branch | remove Cycles assumptions; test probes/reflection plane |
| denoiser erases sparkle | sampling/post | raw EXR | raise samples; separate feature AOV; compare denoiser |
| more resolution changes paint identity | LOD/filtering | projected-size AOV | fix camera metadata and smooth convergence |
| official chip matches but car does not | target/reference | UI proxy versus render | classify finish and fit angular response |
| Weighted Normal “fixes” one shot only | topology/normal | mirror and deviation | correct mesh or restrict modifier scope |

---

# CP12-OEM-NODE-CROSSWALK — Factory option to active graph

| OEM finish class | Required node/data modules | Principal experiments |
|---|---|---|
| uni/solid gloss | Coordinate, Groundcoat, Clearcoat, Coat Topography | solid baseline, DOI |
| fine metallic | Cell Seed, Tangent Frame, Flake Orientation, LOD, Coat | EXP-006, 010, 030 |
| coarse metallic | larger/discrete field, source-size test, stronger LOD | EXP-013, 016, geometry patch |
| mica/pearl | finite groundcoat, angular colour, grain, coat | pearl LUT, tri-coat |
| colourstream/interference | multi-angle LUT/interference branch, illuminant tests | EXP-043, 048 |
| tinted clear/candy | absorbing layer, substrate, coat | EXP-045, 047, thickness ladder |
| matte/Frozen | coat roughness/haze, effect attenuation validation | EXP-030, 105 |
| two-tone | two complete paint identities, mask/process boundary | EXP-109 |
| gradient/ombré | process fields, multiple layer identities, transition | EXP-110 |
| object-matched bespoke | capture, metamerism, identity record | EXP-108 |
| historical reissue | identity/version/formulation comparison | EXP-101, 104 |
| PPF/wrap | protective film stack and edge geometry | EXP-094, 096 |

# CP12-REFERENCE-POLICY — Canonical source record

Every new source entry should use:

```json
{
  "reference_id": null,
  "title": null,
  "authors_or_publisher": null,
  "year_or_version": null,
  "source_type": null,
  "authority_role": null,
  "doi": null,
  "stable_url": null,
  "access_date": null,
  "access_status": null,
  "supports_claims": [],
  "does_not_establish": [],
  "engine_or_material_scope": null,
  "archive_or_snapshot": null,
  "license_or_access_notes": null
}
```

## Source-role vocabulary

- official software documentation;
- official source code;
- open specification;
- standard;
- peer-reviewed research;
- technical report/preprint;
- OEM official;
- refinish/pigment industry;
- current reporting;
- community observation;
- commercial tool discovery;
- historical archive.

## Citation rule inside the master

Use canonical IDs in active technical sections:

```text
[REF-BL-BUMP]
[REF-OPENPBR-001]
[REF-CG-NDFF-2016]
```

Raw URLs remain preserved for searchability, but the reference ID carries meaning.

---

# CP12-PRACTICAL-SCRIPTS — Corrected implementation templates

These are stronger templates than the V10 examples, but remain `HYP` until executed in Blender 5.0.1.

## Camera projection metadata

```python
from __future__ import annotations

import math
import bpy

def effective_resolution(scene: bpy.types.Scene) -> tuple[float, float]:
    scale = scene.render.resolution_percentage / 100.0
    return (
        scene.render.resolution_x * scale,
        scene.render.resolution_y * scale,
    )

def focal_pixels(camera: bpy.types.Camera, scene: bpy.types.Scene) -> dict[str, float]:
    width_px, height_px = effective_resolution(scene)

    angle_x = float(camera.angle_x)
    angle_y = float(camera.angle_y)

    if angle_x <= 0.0 or angle_y <= 0.0:
        raise ValueError("Camera field of view is invalid.")

    return {
        "focal_px_x": 0.5 * width_px / math.tan(0.5 * angle_x),
        "focal_px_y": 0.5 * height_px / math.tan(0.5 * angle_y),
        "effective_width_px": width_px,
        "effective_height_px": height_px,
        "pixel_aspect_x": scene.render.pixel_aspect_x,
        "pixel_aspect_y": scene.render.pixel_aspect_y,
        "camera_type": camera.type,
        "ortho_scale": float(camera.ortho_scale),
    }

scene = bpy.context.scene
camera_object = scene.camera
if camera_object is None or camera_object.type != "CAMERA":
    raise RuntimeError("Scene has no active camera object.")

print(focal_pixels(camera_object.data, scene))
```

This calculates camera constants. It does not provide exact per-shading-point footprint.

## Safer active-material access

```python
import bpy

obj = bpy.context.active_object
if obj is None:
    raise RuntimeError("No active object.")

material = obj.active_material
if material is None or not material.use_nodes:
    raise RuntimeError("Active object has no node-based active material.")

node_tree = material.node_tree
```

## Corrected node audit semantics

```python
from __future__ import annotations

import bpy

REQUIREMENTS = {
    "ShaderNodeBsdfPrincipled": {
        "required_input_names": {"Base Color", "Roughness", "Normal"},
    },
    "ShaderNodeBump": {
        "required_input_names": {"Height", "Normal"},
    },
}

def audit_tree(tree: bpy.types.NodeTree) -> list[dict]:
    findings: list[dict] = []

    for node_type, rules in REQUIREMENTS.items():
        matching = [node for node in tree.nodes if node.bl_idname == node_type]

        if not matching:
            findings.append({
                "severity": "ERROR",
                "node_type": node_type,
                "message": "Required node type not found.",
            })
            continue

        required = set(rules["required_input_names"])

        for node in matching:
            available = {socket.name for socket in node.inputs}
            missing = sorted(required - available)

            if missing:
                findings.append({
                    "severity": "WARNING",
                    "node": node.name,
                    "node_type": node_type,
                    "missing_inputs": missing,
                    "available_inputs": sorted(available),
                })

    return findings
```

The old `inputs_any` name was misleading because the code required every listed name across the union of nodes.

## World-space scene manifest correction

Record:

```python
camera_world_matrix = [list(row) for row in camera_object.matrix_world]
```

rather than only local `location` and `rotation_euler`, which become misleading for parented or constrained cameras.

A complete manifest should additionally include:

- pixel aspect;
- `fps_base`;
- effective resolution;
- sensor fit;
- `angle_x` and `angle_y`;
- orthographic scale;
- shutter/motion-blur settings;
- sample counts;
- adaptive sampling;
- denoiser;
- device/backend;
- AOV list;
- colour-management/OCIO configuration;
- world-node inventory;
- light dimensions and transforms;
- relevant addon versions;
- GPU and driver where obtainable.

---

# CP12-REFERENCE-AUDIT-RULES

## Dynamic official pages

For OEM/configurator sources, store:

- market;
- model;
- model year;
- language;
- access date;
- screenshot;
- saved source/response where lawful;
- checksum;
- archive URL when available.

## Standards

Do not imply the full standard was read when only the abstract/title was available.

Use:

```text
access_status = identifier_verified / abstract_opened / full_text_opened / paywalled
```

## Papers

Prefer:

1. DOI;
2. publisher page;
3. author/institution PDF;
4. arXiv/preprint;
5. secondary summary.

## Blender

Use, in order:

1. exact version manual;
2. release note;
3. source code at a pinned commit;
4. local Blender socket dump;
5. observed render.

## Community and commercial sources

Always label the role:

- workflow discovery;
- reported bug/behavior;
- addon capability;
- artist observation.

They do not establish physical law.

## Broken-link protocol

Never delete the reference.

Append:

- broken date;
- last known title;
- archived URL;
- replacement;
- whether claim confidence changes.

---
