## ABR2-MAT-PC-LENS — Hard-coated polycarbonate lamp cover

**Family:** transparent coated polymer

**Substrate:** optical polycarbonate

**Manufacturing route:** injection moulding, hardcoat/UV coating and assembly

### Layer stack
- hardcoat
- polycarbonate bulk
- inner surface

### Optical/appearance behavior
- transparent dielectric
- bulk absorption
- hardcoat reflection
- stress/mould variation
- weathering haze/yellowing

### Geometry requirements
- true inner/outer thickness
- moulded ribs/flanges
- edge and seal

### Blender representation
- solid transparent geometry
- hardcoat layer
- age/pit/scratch state

### Common failure signatures
- glass preset
- single zero-thickness face
- yellow Base Color only

### Validation
- fresh/aged
- hardcoat ablation
- edge refraction

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-PC-LENS]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-PMMA — PMMA/acrylic lamp optic

**Family:** transparent polymer

**Substrate:** PMMA

**Manufacturing route:** injection moulding or casting with optical features

### Layer stack
- optional hardcoat
- PMMA bulk

### Optical/appearance behavior
- high clarity
- different toughness/weathering from PC
- bulk colour possible

### Geometry requirements
- prisms/light-guide geometry
- true thickness

### Blender representation
- transparent solid
- absorption
- micro-optic geometry/bake

### Common failure signatures
- identical to polycarbonate without classification
- surface-only refraction

### Validation
- PC/PMMA comparison
- microprism LOD

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-PC-LENS]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-PA-GF — Glass-filled nylon technical component

**Family:** short-fibre-reinforced thermoplastic

**Substrate:** polyamide with short glass fibres

**Manufacturing route:** injection moulding with flow-induced fibre orientation

### Layer stack
- moulded surface
- fibre-filled bulk

### Optical/appearance behavior
- subtle fibre/flow texture
- semi-matte technical appearance
- stress whitening

### Geometry requirements
- ribs, bosses, fastener seats
- flow/weld lines

### Blender representation
- bulk polymer
- directional flow field
- subtle fibre exposure

### Common failure signatures
- woven fibreglass
- random metallic fibres

### Validation
- flow direction
- weld-line region
- wear exposure

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-ABS-PCABS]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-EPDM — EPDM weather seal

**Family:** elastomer

**Substrate:** EPDM rubber

**Manufacturing route:** extrusion/moulding, splicing, flocking/coating where present

### Layer stack
- surface treatment/contamination
- rubber bulk

### Optical/appearance behavior
- dark absorbing body
- soft dielectric reflection
- extrusion texture
- contact polish

### Geometry requirements
- compressed lip
- hollow section
- joint/splice
- contact with glass/body

### Blender representation
- elastic-looking geometry
- rubber shader
- compression and dust fields

### Common failure signatures
- flat black strip
- no deformation/contact
- same shader as tyre

### Validation
- door open/closed compression
- wet/dry
- aged crack

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-RUBBER-WEAR]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-TYRE-TREAD — Tyre tread compound

**Family:** filled elastomer composite

**Substrate:** tread rubber compound with carbon black/silica and additives

**Manufacturing route:** mixing, extrusion, tyre building, moulding/vulcanisation and wear

### Layer stack
- surface bloom/dirt/water
- worn rubber tread
- bulk tread

### Optical/appearance behavior
- dark but not zero-reflectance
- mould texture
- abrasion polish
- embedded debris

### Geometry requirements
- real tread blocks/grooves/sipes
- contact-patch deformation
- wear bars

### Blender representation
- tread geometry
- rubber shader
- wear/temperature/wetness
- rotation-safe detail

### Common failure signatures
- flat black
- no contact flattening
- sidewall and tread identical
- random noise tread

### Validation
- new/worn/wet/hot
- contact patch
- rotation temporal

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-RUBBER-WEAR]
- [REF-ABR-RUBBER-WEAR-2025]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-TYRE-SIDE — Tyre sidewall compound and markings

**Family:** filled elastomer composite

**Substrate:** sidewall rubber and lettering compounds

**Manufacturing route:** moulding/vulcanisation, embossing, printed or coloured markings

### Layer stack
- dressing/dirt/bloom
- sidewall compound
- raised lettering

### Optical/appearance behavior
- different gloss/texture from tread
- fine mould grain
- embossed text

### Geometry requirements
- bulge
- bead
- lettering
- vent spews
- seam

### Blender representation
- separate sidewall material
- real/displaced lettering
- age/dressing

### Common failure signatures
- same roughness as tread
- flat normal-map text at macro
- no load bulge

### Validation
- loaded/unloaded
- new/aged/dressed

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-RUBBER-WEAR]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-WINDSCREEN — Laminated automotive windscreen

**Family:** laminated transparent composite

**Substrate:** two glass plies with PVB or equivalent interlayer

**Manufacturing route:** glass forming, printing/coating, lamination under heat/pressure, edge finishing

### Layer stack
- outer coatings/contamination
- outer glass
- interlayer
- inner glass
- inner coatings

### Optical/appearance behavior
- multiple dielectric interfaces
- bulk/interlayer tint
- possible wedge/HUD
- polarization and stress effects
- edge darkening

### Geometry requirements
- true curved inner/outer surfaces
- thickness
- edge
- frit
- seal

### Blender representation
- solid glass layers or validated simplification
- absorption
- frit
- wires/sensors

### Common failure signatures
- single plane
- uniform dark tint
- no inner reflection
- floating glass

### Validation
- solid/thin comparison
- grazing reflection
- interior/exterior view
- EEVEE/Cycles

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-PVB]
- [REF-ABR-LSG-BUBBLE]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-TEMPERED — Tempered side/rear glass

**Family:** transparent safety glass

**Substrate:** thermally tempered glass

**Manufacturing route:** forming and tempering with optional tint/coatings

### Layer stack
- surface coatings
- glass bulk

### Optical/appearance behavior
- dielectric reflection/transmission
- bulk tint
- stress pattern possible under polarization

### Geometry requirements
- true thickness
- edge profile
- heating wires for rear glass

### Blender representation
- solid glass
- absorption
- optional stress/polarization reference only

### Common failure signatures
- laminated stack on all side glass
- surface-only tint

### Validation
- laminated/tempered comparison
- edge view

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-LAMINATED-GLASS]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-SOLARGLASS — Solar-control coated automotive glass

**Family:** coated transparent glazing

**Substrate:** laminated or tempered glass

**Manufacturing route:** bulk tint and/or deposited solar-control coating

### Layer stack
- functional coating
- glass/interlayer stack

### Optical/appearance behavior
- visible tint
- IR/UV control
- angle-dependent reflected colour possible

### Geometry requirements
- same glazing requirements as base glass

### Blender representation
- glass stack
- subtle thin-film/colour layer
- spectral limitation label

### Common failure signatures
- strong rainbow interference without evidence
- opaque dark glass

### Validation
- several illuminants
- angle sweep
- interior visibility

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-LAMINATED-GLASS]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-FRIT — Ceramic frit and dot matrix

**Family:** printed ceramic coating on glass

**Substrate:** glass

**Manufacturing route:** screen printing ceramic ink and firing/forming

### Layer stack
- ceramic printed region
- glass

### Optical/appearance behavior
- opaque/semi-opaque black region
- halftone dot transition
- different surface reflection

### Geometry requirements
- exact OEM pattern
- dot scale
- edge relation to adhesive/seal

### Blender representation
- UV/decal pattern
- optional macro geometry
- glass integration

### Common failure signatures
- random dots
- screen-space pattern
- wrong scale

### Validation
- macro edge
- pattern accuracy
- moiré/LOD

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-LAMINATED-GLASS]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-REFLECTOR — Metallised lamp reflector

**Family:** optical metallised polymer

**Substrate:** precision moulded polymer

**Manufacturing route:** optical moulding, basecoat and vacuum metal deposition

### Layer stack
- protective layer
- aluminium-like metal film
- basecoat
- polymer

### Optical/appearance behavior
- high-reflectance image-forming surface
- microfacet quality depends on mould and coating

### Geometry requirements
- true reflector/freeform facets
- mounting and cutoff details
- no coarse polygon faceting

### Blender representation
- reflective metal layer
- exact geometry
- age/burn/dust

### Common failure signatures
- chrome sphere material on crude mesh
- uniform emission

### Validation
- beam-forming reference
- moving highlight
- mirror geometry diagnostic

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-METALLIZING]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-LIGHTGUIDE — Automotive polymer light guide

**Family:** transparent guided-light optic

**Substrate:** PMMA or PC optical guide

**Manufacturing route:** precision moulding with extraction features and LED coupling

### Layer stack
- surface/hardcoat if present
- transparent guide
- micro extraction features

### Optical/appearance behavior
- total internal reflection
- absorption
- scattering/extraction
- source coupling

### Geometry requirements
- solid guide
- LED injection face
- prisms/dots
- bends

### Blender representation
- hero transport branch
- baked distribution branch
- emission only as declared approximation

### Common failure signatures
- uniform emissive tube
- no source gradient
- bloom hiding geometry

### Validation
- source-off/on
- extraction-density ladder
- Cycles/EEVEE

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-AUTO-LIGHTING]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-DIFFUSER — Lamp diffuser/lens texture

**Family:** microstructured transparent/translucent polymer

**Substrate:** PC/PMMA

**Manufacturing route:** moulding of prisms, lenticules, texture or scattering particles

### Layer stack
- surface microstructure
- polymer bulk

### Optical/appearance behavior
- controlled angular redistribution
- transmission haze
- colour from lamp state

### Geometry requirements
- real microprisms for macro or baked normal/BTDF proxy

### Blender representation
- normal/geometry LOD
- transmission roughness
- baked lamp response

### Common failure signatures
- white translucent plastic
- random noise bump

### Validation
- macro/distance
- off-state realism
- beam pattern proxy

### Reference links
- [REF-ABR-AUTO-LIGHTING]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-PPF — Gloss paint-protection film

**Family:** transparent adhesive film

**Substrate:** factory paint

**Manufacturing route:** polyurethane film manufacture, adhesive application and vehicle installation

### Layer stack
- film topcoat
- polyurethane film
- adhesive
- factory clearcoat and paint

### Optical/appearance behavior
- additional interfaces
- film texture
- possible slight tint/haze
- edge/seam

### Geometry requirements
- real film edge for macro
- wrapped panel boundary
- lift/bubble defects

- thin shell or layer approximation
- surface texture
- edge geometry
- underlying paint preserved

- no edge
- changing underlying paint identity

### Validation

- grazing strip
- edge macro

### Reference links
- [REF-OPENPBR-001]

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


## ABR2-MAT-VINYL — Coloured vinyl vehicle wrap

**Family:** pigmented adhesive polymer film

**Substrate:** painted body

**Manufacturing route:** cast/calendered film, printing where relevant, adhesive installation and heat forming

### Layer stack
- protective laminate where present
- pigmented/printed vinyl
- adhesive
- paint

### Optical/appearance behavior
- polymer surface
- lower DOI than paint possible
- stretch-induced texture
- seams

### Geometry requirements
- film edges
- overlaps
- relief cuts
- stretch field

### Blender representation
- film layer
- strain/UV distortion
- edge geometry
- condition

### Common failure signatures

- no seams/stretch
- chrome wrap treated as solid metal

### Validation

- seam macro
- paint/wrap comparison

## ABR2-MAT-BADGE — Plated plastic automotive badge

**Family:** multimaterial emblem

**Substrate:** moulded plastic badge body

**Manufacturing route:** moulding, plating/painting, enamel or printed infill, adhesive assembly

### Layer stack
- metal/plating
- infill/dome
- plastic body
- adhesive foam

### Optical/appearance behavior
- bright metal border
- coloured resin/enamel
- small-scale bevel reflections

### Geometry requirements
- true relief
- bevel
- backing gap
- adhesive thickness

### Blender representation

- bevel geometry
- enamel meniscus
- damage

- one metallic material
- infinitely sharp letter edges

- adhesive contact

### Reference links

**Initial status:** `HYP` unless an existing embedded source gives a higher evidence state.


# Part XXVIII — Manufacturing-process cards


## ABR2-PROC-001 — Prepreg/autoclave composite

Pre-impregnated reinforcement is laid into a mould, vacuum-bagged and cured under controlled temperature and pressure.

### Controlling variables
- ply orientation
- debulk quality
- resin content
- tool surface
- cure cycle

### Appearance evidence
- cosmetic ply alignment
- seams
- resin-rich corners
- print-through
- pinholes

### Blender linkage
- GN ply/seam fields
- clearcoat/resin separation
- print-through height bands

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- woven pattern alone cannot identify autoclave quality

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-002 — Resin-transfer moulding

Dry reinforcement is placed in a closed mould and resin is injected through the preform.

### Controlling variables
- flow front
- permeability
- venting
- resin viscosity
- mould face

### Appearance evidence
- dry spots
- resin pools
- fibre wash
- mould-face finish

### Blender linkage
- flow-field masks
- resin-richness
- defect localisation

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- do not randomise defects uniformly

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-003 — Vacuum-assisted resin infusion

Vacuum draws resin through dry reinforcement and consumable stack.

### Controlling variables
- inlet/vent layout
- flow media
- vacuum quality
- fibre compaction

### Appearance evidence
- race tracking
- dry areas
- print-through
- variable resin

### Blender linkage
- process-direction fields
- edge race masks

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- reference the actual part before adding defects

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-004 — Compression moulding of CF-SMC

Chopped-tow sheet charges flow under heat and pressure in a matched die.

### Controlling variables
- charge placement
- flow distance
- tool temperature
- pressure
- weld fronts

### Appearance evidence
- bundle flow orientation
- resin pockets
- weld lines
- mould texture

### Blender linkage
- GN flow simulation proxy
- bundle field
- weld-front collision masks

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- forged carbon is not isotropic Voronoi

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-005 — Composite draping

A textile or prepreg ply shears and conforms over a doubly curved tool.

### Controlling variables
- warp/weft direction
- shear angle
- slip
- wrinkle onset
- cut pattern

### Appearance evidence
- distorted weave
- wrinkles
- tow spreading
- seams

### Blender linkage
- direction fields
- UV shear diagnostics
- cloth/drape bake

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- rigid planar texture is invalid on complex curvature

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-006 — Composite sanding and clear finishing

Cured composite is filled/sanded and clearcoated or polished for cosmetic use.

### Controlling variables
- sand grit
- filler
- clear thickness
- polish
- edge burn-through

### Appearance evidence
- sanding marks
- DOI
- resin exposure
- edge damage

### Blender linkage
- coat-normal scratches
- surface-condition states

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- do not put sanding into fibre topology

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-007 — Decorative nickel-chromium plating

Surface preparation and one or more nickel layers create brightness/corrosion performance before thin chromium.

### Controlling variables
- polish
- nickel structure
- chromium thickness
- substrate
- pores/cracks

### Appearance evidence
- mirror finish
- haze
- pitting
- peeling

### Blender linkage
- layered conductor proxy
- substrate-specific damage

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- chrome appearance is not only chromium

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-008 — Plating on plastics

Plastic is etched/activated, made conductive and electroplated.

### Controlling variables
- mould surface
- etch
- activation
- copper/nickel/chrome
- thermal mismatch

### Appearance evidence
- mould fingerprints
- blistering
- edge peel

### Blender linkage
- plastic-derived geometry
- plating stack
- peel masks

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- damage must reveal polymer

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-009 — Vacuum metallisation

Metal vapour condenses as a very thin film on a prepared substrate.

### Controlling variables
- basecoat smoothness
- vacuum
- metal
- topcoat
- substrate outgassing

### Appearance evidence
- high reflection
- topcoat haze
- burn/oxidation
- peel

### Blender linkage
- thin metal + clear
- mould geometry

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- not equivalent to bulk aluminium

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-010 — PVD/sputtered trim coating

A thin deposited layer is applied in vacuum, often over base and under topcoat.

### Controlling variables
- target material
- layer thickness
- ion energy
- substrate prep
- topcoat

### Appearance evidence
- bright/dark chrome
- colour
- scratch-through

### Blender linkage
- thin-film/conductor stack
- topcoat

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- do not guess exact spectral data

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-011 — Mechanical polishing

Successive abrasive stages reduce roughness and create directional or isotropic residual marks.

### Controlling variables
- abrasive size
- pad path
- pressure
- substrate
- final compound

### Appearance evidence
- micro-scratches
- haze
- swirl
- edge rounding

### Blender linkage
- multiscale Gabor/scratch field
- process path

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- perfect smooth metal is rarely reference-accurate

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-012 — Linear brushing

Abrasive contact creates predominantly parallel grooves.

### Controlling variables
- belt direction
- grit
- feed
- cross-brush
- edge turn

### Appearance evidence
- anisotropy
- stroke hierarchy
- cross scratches

### Blender linkage
- tangent field
- anisotropic BSDF
- Gabor

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- one-frequency lines are inadequate

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-013 — Bead blasting

Particles impact the surface and produce stochastic dimples/roughness.

### Controlling variables
- media size
- pressure
- angle
- coverage
- substrate

### Appearance evidence
- isotropic broad highlight
- peening texture

### Blender linkage
- band-limited normal/roughness

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- do not use giant craters

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-014 — Single-point/diamond machining

A cutting tool generates deterministic feed marks on a metal surface.

### Controlling variables
- tool radius
- feed
- spindle path
- material
- subsequent clearcoat

### Appearance evidence
- concentric/spiral grooves
- sharp directional glints

### Blender linkage
- radial coordinate
- groove LOD
- clearcoat

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- linear brush map is wrong

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-015 — Anodising

Electrochemical oxide growth creates a porous protective layer that may be dyed and sealed.

### Controlling variables
- pre-finish
- oxide thickness
- dye
- seal
- alloy

### Appearance evidence
- colour
- metal/oxide interaction
- edge wear

### Blender linkage
- metal substrate + dielectric/colour layer

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- opaque paint model is incomplete

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-016 — Powder coating

Electrostatically deposited powder is fused/cured into a polymer coating.

### Controlling variables
- particle size
- film build
- cure
- substrate
- texture formulation

### Appearance evidence
- orange peel
- edge coverage
- gloss family

### Blender linkage
- coated-metal stack
- film texture

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- not the same as wet automotive paint

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-017 — Injection moulding

Molten polymer fills a mould, cools and is ejected.

### Controlling variables
- gate
- flow front
- cooling
- pressure
- mould texture
- wall thickness

### Appearance evidence
- flow lines
- weld lines
- sink
- parting lines
- gloss variation

### Blender linkage
- GN process fields
- mould texture
- part-specific geometry

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- random dirt is not a mould defect

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-018 — Mould graining/texture

The tool surface is etched, engraved or otherwise textured and transfers the negative pattern to the part.

### Controlling variables
- tool texture
- draft
- replication fidelity
- polymer shrink

### Appearance evidence
- repeatable areal texture
- directionality
- local stretch

### Blender linkage
- physical-scale procedural/baked height
- UV/object coordinates

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- generic Noise ignores tool origin

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-019 — Painting flexible plastics

Prepared polymer receives promoter/primer/base/clear under a different process window from metal bodies.

### Controlling variables
- surface energy
- primer
- flexibility
- cure temperature
- separate line

### Appearance evidence
- colour/gloss mismatch
- orange peel
- adhesion failure

### Blender linkage
- Paint V12 trim variant
- process ID

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- copying body material exactly may be wrong

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-020 — Polycarbonate hardcoating

A transparent protective layer improves scratch and weathering resistance.

### Controlling variables
- coating chemistry
- thickness
- UV exposure
- adhesion
- cure

### Appearance evidence
- surface reflection
- scratch resistance
- haze/yellowing on failure

### Blender linkage
- transparent substrate + hardcoat + age

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- yellow tint alone is not ageing

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-021 — Glass lamination

Glass plies and polymer interlayer are assembled, de-aired and bonded under heat and pressure.

### Controlling variables
- interlayer moisture
- air removal
- temperature
- pressure
- edge seal

### Appearance evidence
- multiple interfaces
- bubbles
- delamination

### Blender linkage
- solid layered glazing
- defect masks

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- single surface omits laminate

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-022 — Glass tempering

Thermal treatment creates surface compression and fracture behaviour.

### Controlling variables
- heat profile
- quench
- shape
- edge quality

### Appearance evidence
- stress patterns under polarisation
- optical distortion

### Blender linkage
- geometry/normal accuracy
- optional diagnostic stress proxy

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- do not invent visible rainbow in ordinary views

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-023 — Ceramic frit printing

Ceramic ink is printed on glass and fired during processing.

### Controlling variables
- screen pattern
- ink
- firing
- dot gradient

### Appearance evidence
- opaque border
- dot matrix
- surface difference

### Blender linkage
- OEM UV/decal pattern

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- random dots are inaccurate

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-024 — Optical polymer moulding

Precision moulding forms headlamp lenses, prisms and light guides.

### Controlling variables
- tool polish
- shrinkage
- birefringence/stress
- feature fidelity

### Appearance evidence
- high-DOI surfaces
- microprisms
- flow/stress

### Blender linkage
- true solid geometry + microfeature LOD

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- rough glass preset is inadequate

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-025 — LED light-guide extraction

Microfeatures progressively extract guided light to achieve target uniformity.

### Controlling variables
- feature density
- source coupling
- guide curvature
- absorption

### Appearance evidence
- brightness gradient
- hot spots
- off-state pattern

### Blender linkage
- geometry/bake/emission hierarchy

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- uniform emission hides design

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-026 — Tyre vulcanisation and moulding

Built tyre assembly is cured in a mould with tread, sidewall and lettering features.

### Controlling variables
- compound
- mould
- pressure
- temperature
- venting

### Appearance evidence
- mould grain
- seam
- vent spews
- lettering

### Blender linkage
- real tread/letter geometry
- material separation

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- flat black cylinder fails

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-027 — Brake-disc machining and bedding

Friction surfaces are machined and then develop pad-transfer/wear films.

### Controlling variables
- surface finish
- pad
- temperature
- usage

### Appearance evidence
- radial tracks
- transfer film
- heat spots

### Blender linkage
- rotation coordinates
- usage state

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- rust and wear cannot be random

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-028 — C/SiC silicon infiltration

A carbon preform is infiltrated with silicon that reacts to form silicon carbide phases.

### Controlling variables
- preform
- porosity
- silicon
- reaction
- friction layer

### Appearance evidence
- ceramic composite texture
- surface finish
- wear

### Blender linkage
- nonwoven composite + ceramic matrix

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- twill carbon texture is false

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-029 — Wheel casting

Molten alloy fills a mould and is heat-treated/machined/finished.

### Controlling variables
- mould
- porosity
- machining
- paint

### Appearance evidence
- cast texture where exposed
- edge/form differences

### Blender linkage
- wheel geometry + finish stack

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- forged and cast forms are not interchangeable

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


## ABR2-PROC-030 — Wheel forging/flow forming

Material is plastically formed under high load or spun to refine geometry and properties.

### Controlling variables
- blank
- die
- flow
- machining

### Appearance evidence
- thin strong sections
- machined finish
- different design cues

### Blender linkage
- geometry-first classification

### Anti-hallucination boundary
- shader cannot create forged geometry

**Status:** process-informed `INFERENCE/HYP` until target-specific evidence is attached.


# Part XXIX — Exact node and Geometry Nodes group specifications


## ABR2-NODE-001 — `CF_WEAVE_DRAFT_B50`

**Domain:** Shader/GN hybrid

### Inputs
- warp coordinate
- weft coordinate
- weave type
- tow count
- gap
- phase
- drape directions

### Outputs
- warp mask
- weft mask
- over-under state
- tow centre distance
- gap mask
- crimp height

### Active construction logic
- plain: parity alternates every crossing
- 2x2 twill: two-over/two-under diagonal progression
- spread tow: wide flat tows and low crimp

### Diagnostics
- pattern-ID AOV
- over-under AOV
- cell coordinate
- physical-pitch checker

### Prohibitions
- no random topology
- no camera coordinates
- no Generated scale for final assets

**Status:** `HYP`; exact Blender 5.0.1 node/socket validation required.


## ABR2-NODE-002 — `CF_FIBRE_MICRO_B50`

**Domain:** Shader

### Inputs
- fibre direction
- tow mask
- micro scale
- bandwidth
- amplitude
- resin coverage

### Outputs
- fibre colour variation
- directional normal
- directional roughness
- microstructure AOV

### Active construction logic
- directional Gabor/texture aligned to local tow direction
- filtered amplitude decreases before aliasing

### Diagnostics
- encoded direction
- frequency AOV
- normal magnitude

### Prohibitions
- not metallic by default
- not visible as white hairs

**Status:** `HYP`; exact Blender 5.0.1 node/socket validation required.


## ABR2-NODE-003 — `CF_DRAPE_FIELD_GN_B50`

**Domain:** Geometry Nodes

### Inputs
- surface
- warp guide curves
- weft guide/derived field
- seams
- reference scale

### Outputs
- stored warp
- stored weft
- shear angle
- seam distance
- confidence

### Active construction logic
- sample/transfer direction fields to mesh corners or points
- re-orthogonalise only if the intended textile remains orthogonal; otherwise preserve shear

### Diagnostics
- direction-colour viewport
- shear heatmap
- seam overlay

### Prohibitions
- field discontinuities must be deliberate

**Status:** `HYP`; exact Blender 5.0.1 node/socket validation required.


## ABR2-NODE-004 — `CF_FORGED_BUNDLES_B50`

**Domain:** Shader/GN

### Inputs
- position mm
- bundle density
- length distribution
- width distribution
- orientation field
- depth populations
- seed

### Outputs
- bundle ID
- local coordinate
- coverage
- orientation
- depth
- resin gap

### Active construction logic
- Poisson/Voronoi only seeds bundle centres; local elongated masks create bundles
- overlap several layers

### Diagnostics
- bundle ID
- orientation hue
- length/width AOV

### Prohibitions
- no visible Voronoi cells
- no identical shards

**Status:** `HYP`; exact Blender 5.0.1 node/socket validation required.


## ABR2-NODE-005 — `METAL_PROCESS_FRAME_B50`

**Domain:** Shader/GN

### Inputs
- surface normal
- process direction
- radial centre/axis
- mode

### Outputs
- tangent
- bitangent
- radial coordinate
- azimuth
- process diagnostic

### Active construction logic
- linear brushing uses stored direction
- machined wheel uses radial/tangential frame
- weld uses curve-distance frame

### Diagnostics
- orthogonality
- direction colour
- seam mask

### Prohibitions
- world-space direction only if process truly world-defined

**Status:** `HYP`; exact Blender 5.0.1 node/socket validation required.


## ABR2-NODE-006 — `METAL_BRUSH_B50`

**Domain:** Shader

### Inputs
- process frame
- stroke scale
- bandwidth
- cross-scratch rate
- roughness base
- age

### Outputs
- anisotropy rotation
- roughness
- normal
- deep scratch mask

### Active construction logic
- multiband Gabor for fine strokes
- separate sparse long scratches
- filter by LOD

### Diagnostics
- fine/coarse band AOVs
- scratch mask

### Prohibitions
- one Wave texture is not sufficient

**Status:** `HYP`; exact Blender 5.0.1 node/socket validation required.


## ABR2-NODE-007 — `METAL_PLATING_STACK_B50`

**Domain:** Shader

### Inputs
- substrate class
- metal colour
- underlayer tint
- topcoat
- roughness
- damage

### Outputs
- surface BSDF
- layer exposure masks
- plating diagnostic

### Active construction logic
- default to physically layered or fitted approximation
- damage reveals correct substrate

### Diagnostics
- topcoat weight
- metal layer
- substrate mask

### Prohibitions
- do not add arbitrary chrome emission

**Status:** `HYP`; exact Blender 5.0.1 node/socket validation required.


## ABR2-NODE-008 — `METAL_HEAT_OXIDE_B50`

**Domain:** Shader

### Inputs
- base metal
- temperature/history field
- oxide thickness proxy
- soot
- roughness

### Outputs
- thin-film colour
- base conductor
- heat AOV

### Active construction logic
- map process history to film thickness; use Thin Film/LUT only within declared scope

### Diagnostics
- history field
- film thickness
- soot

### Prohibitions
- no rainbow ramp disconnected from process

## ABR2-NODE-009 — `PLASTIC_MOULD_SURFACE_B50`

### Inputs
- position mm
- surface class
- texture spectrum
- flow direction
- gloss
- wear

### Outputs

- normal
- roughness

- use measured-scale multi-band fields and areal-statistic targets

- flow

### Inputs

- primer
- paint/plating/topcoat mode
- damage depth

- exposure masks

- branch by actual process: uncoated, painted, piano-black, plated, PVD

- one plastic group cannot hide all stacks

- inner glass

- hero Cycles uses solid nested interfaces; production tier may collapse layers after comparison

# Part XXX — Deep Blender modifier cards


## ABR2-MOD-001 — Weighted Normal

**Actual role:** custom corner-normal adjustment

### Strong uses
- large planar hard-surface faces
- bevelled trim
- scoped vertex-group regions

### Dangerous misuse
- body-form repair
- removing triangulation
- fixing silhouette

### Parameters/data that must be recorded
- weighting mode
- Keep Sharp
- Face Influence
- vertex group
- modifier order

### Mandatory tests
- mirror material
- true/shading-normal angle
- moving strip
- rotation

### Reference
- [REF-BL50-WEIGHTED-NORMAL]

**Acceptance rule:** modifier remains non-destructive until the result passes geometry and shading validation.


## ABR2-MOD-002 — Data Transfer

**Actual role:** transfer/interpolate mesh data

### Strong uses
- custom normals from source/reference
- UVs
- colour/named attributes
- material-related masks

### Dangerous misuse
- blind nearest mapping across adjacent panels
- topology repair

### Parameters/data that must be recorded
- source object
- data type
- mapping mode
- max distance
- ray radius
- mix mode

### Mandatory tests
- distance heatmap
- seam check
- fresh-file dependency

### Reference
- [REF-BL50-DATA-TRANSFER]

**Acceptance rule:** modifier remains non-destructive until the result passes geometry and shading validation.


## ABR2-MOD-003 — Smooth by Angle

**Actual role:** mark/use smoothing boundaries by face angle

### Strong uses
- mechanical parts
- initial sharp-boundary classification

### Dangerous misuse
- Class-A body repair
- creating bevels

### Parameters/data that must be recorded
- angle
- ignore sharpness where applicable
- GN asset dependency

### Mandatory tests
- boundary overlay
- mirror

### Reference
- [REF-BL50-SMOOTH-ANGLE]

**Acceptance rule:** modifier remains non-destructive until the result passes geometry and shading validation.


## ABR2-MOD-004 — Normal Edit

**Actual role:** generated radial/directional custom normals

### Strong uses
- special radial parts
- deliberate reflection cheat
- architectural/game-style shading

### Dangerous misuse
- universal body smoothing
- accurate curvature reconstruction

### Parameters/data that must be recorded
- mode
- target
- parallel/radial direction
- mix
- vertex group

### Mandatory tests
- object rotation
- reflection line
- target dependency

### Reference
- [REF-BL50-NORMAL-EDIT]

**Acceptance rule:** modifier remains non-destructive until the result passes geometry and shading validation.


## ABR2-MOD-005 — Subdivision Surface

**Actual role:** Catmull-Clark or Simple refinement

### Strong uses
- clean control cages
- reconstructed trim/body patches
- smooth lenses

### Dangerous misuse
- arbitrary triangulated game mesh
- preserving exact tessellated CAD form automatically

### Parameters/data that must be recorded
- levels
- render levels
- boundary/UV smoothing
- creases
- quality

### Mandatory tests
- limit surface
- silhouette
- panel swelling
- normal continuity

### Reference
- [REF-BL50-SUBDIV]

**Acceptance rule:** modifier remains non-destructive until the result passes geometry and shading validation.


## ABR2-MOD-006 — Triangulate

**Actual role:** deterministic polygon triangulation

### Strong uses
- freeze normal-map/runtime diagonals
- export parity
- comparison

### Dangerous misuse
- early destructive cleanup
- making poor topology good

### Parameters/data that must be recorded
- quad/ngon method
- minimum vertices
- keep normals where available

### Mandatory tests
- normal-map A/B
- diagonal overlay

### Reference
- [REF-BL50-TRIANGULATE]

**Acceptance rule:** modifier remains non-destructive until the result passes geometry and shading validation.


## ABR2-MOD-007 — Bevel

**Actual role:** generate geometric edge radius

### Strong uses
- badges
- metal trim
- fasteners
- panel edges where physically justified

### Dangerous misuse
- uniformly rounding every body edge
- replacing correct panel-gap geometry

### Parameters/data that must be recorded
- width units
- segments
- limit method
- profile
- clamp overlap
- harden normals

### Mandatory tests
- silhouette
- physical radius
- reflection width
- intersections

### Reference
- [REF-BL50-BEVEL]

**Acceptance rule:** modifier remains non-destructive until the result passes geometry and shading validation.


## ABR2-MOD-008 — Weld

**Actual role:** merge vertices within distance

### Strong uses
- duplicate-vertex repair after import
- controlled seam cleanup

### Dangerous misuse
- joining deliberate panel/UV/normal seams
- global large threshold

### Parameters/data that must be recorded
- distance
- vertex group
- loose mode

### Mandatory tests
- vertex/edge counts
- UV seams
- material boundary
- before/after diff

### Reference
- [REF-BL50-WELD]

**Acceptance rule:** modifier remains non-destructive until the result passes geometry and shading validation.


## ABR2-MOD-009 — Shrinkwrap

**Actual role:** project geometry toward target

### Strong uses
- retopology patch
- badge/decal fit
- reference-conforming proxy

### Dangerous misuse
- copying faceting/noise blindly
- using wrong projection direction

### Parameters/data that must be recorded
- mode
- offset
- cull
- project axes
- target

### Mandatory tests
- reference distance
- reflection lines
- self-intersection

### Reference
- [REF-BL50-SHRINKWRAP]

**Acceptance rule:** modifier remains non-destructive until the result passes geometry and shading validation.


## ABR2-MOD-010 — Solidify

**Actual role:** add sheet thickness

### Strong uses
- splitters
- lamp shells
- grilles
- glass proxies after validation

### Dangerous misuse
- full body panel thickness without section check
- nested glass shortcuts

- clamp

- cross-section
- normal orientation
- corner self-intersection

### Reference
- [REF-BL50-SOLIDIFY]

**Acceptance rule:** modifier remains non-destructive until the result passes geometry and shading validation.


## ABR2-MOD-011 — Decimate

**Actual role:** reduce polygon count

### Strong uses
- LOD/proxy generation
- non-critical hidden geometry

### Dangerous misuse
- hero body rehabilitation
- preserving weak highlight features by assumption

### Parameters/data that must be recorded
- mode
- ratio
- symmetry
- delimit

### Mandatory tests
- Hausdorff/nearest distance
- silhouette
- moving strip
- normal map

### Reference
- [REF-CG-CWF-2024]

**Acceptance rule:** modifier remains non-destructive until the result passes geometry and shading validation.


## ABR2-MOD-012 — Remesh

**Actual role:** rebuild topology from volume/surface

- rough collision/reference
- organic debris/damage

### Dangerous misuse
- automotive body highlight surfaces
- UV/material preservation

### Parameters/data that must be recorded
- voxel size
- mode
- smooth

### Mandatory tests
- distance
- feature loss
- panel edges

### Reference
- [REF-BL50-REMESH]

**Acceptance rule:** modifier remains non-destructive until the result passes geometry and shading validation.


## ABR2-MOD-013 — Mirror

# Part XXXI — Part-specific game-asset salvage playbooks


## ABR2-SALV-001 — FBX import

### Preserve and inspect
- record importer settings, axis, scale and animation options
- preserve original hierarchy and transforms
- check custom normals, material slots and multiple UV sets

### Primary risks
- negative scale
- unexpected bone/pivot transforms
- split vertices
- embedded-media paths

### Required evidence
- pristine import file
- world-dimension check
- normal/UV inventory

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-002 — OBJ/MTL import

### Preserve and inspect
- preserve groups, smoothing groups and material names
- interpret MTL extensions cautiously
- expect no hierarchy/animation

### Primary risks
- normal map convention
- vendor-specific MTL keys
- lost units
- split vertices

### Required evidence
- known dimension
- material channel audit
- smoothing comparison

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-003 — glTF/GLB import

### Preserve and inspect
- preserve node hierarchy, primitives, tangents, UV sets and PBR extensions
- record extension list
- retain original triangulation

### Primary risks
- normal-map tangent parity
- unsupported extensions
- colour-space assumptions

### Required evidence
- Khronos validator where applicable
- tangent/normal render

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-004 — Proprietary game export

### Preserve and inspect
- record extractor/importer versions
- preserve raw dumps
- separate technical decoding from artistic interpretation

### Primary risks
- unknown channel packing
- coordinate conversion
- lossy intermediate formats

### Required evidence
- cross-check in original game screenshots
- checksum and tool log

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-005 — Dense triangulated CAD-like body

### Preserve and inspect
- test preserved mesh first
- inspect edge-length distribution and normal quality
- avoid automatic quad conversion

### Primary risks
- faceting from normals rather than geometry
- unnecessary SubD drift

### Required evidence
- mirror/strip test
- distance to reference after any rebuild

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-006 — Low-poly body with baked normals

### Preserve and inspect
- preserve custom normals and normal maps
- identify silhouette limits
- classify hero shot needs

### Primary risks
- recalculated normals destroy intended shading
- SubD inflates body

### Required evidence
- original game-like lighting
- mirror and silhouette

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-007 — Split vertices at UV/material seams

### Preserve and inspect
- identify coincident positions with distinct loop data
- preserve until purpose known

### Primary risks
- Weld destroys tangent seams or panel boundaries

### Required evidence
- vertex cluster report
- UV/normal diff after weld

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-008 — Mirrored object transforms

### Preserve and inspect
- record determinant and axis
- apply transforms only on duplicate
- check tangent handedness

### Primary risks
- inside-out normals
- mirrored text
- normal-map green-channel issues

### Required evidence
- world normal AOV
- text/UV check

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-009 — Imported custom normals

### Preserve and inspect
- render before modification
- export/dump state
- test weighted/recalculated/transferred alternatives

### Primary risks
- custom normals may be unavailable after topology edit

### Required evidence
- moving strip
- angular error AOV

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-010 — Tangent-space normal maps

### Preserve and inspect
- identify UV map and handedness
- freeze triangulation
- use Non-Color and Normal Map node
- test Y+/Y-

### Primary risks
- mismatched MikkTSpace
- mirrored islands
- retriangulation

### Required evidence
- light rotation
- seam inspection

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-011 — Packed ORM/RMA textures

### Preserve and inspect
- identify channel convention from source shader
- split channels explicitly
- record colour space

### Primary risks
- AO used as albedo
- roughness/gloss inversion

### Required evidence
- channel solo previews
- numeric range histogram

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-012 — Gloss/specular legacy workflow

### Preserve and inspect
- determine whether map is glossiness, specular level or colour
- convert with documented assumptions

### Primary risks
- 1-gloss is not universally enough
- metalness conversion loses data

### Required evidence
- material exemplars
- source-engine comparison

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-013 — Vertex-colour masks

### Preserve and inspect
- inventory all colour attributes and domains
- visualise channels
- trace source shader usage

### Primary risks
- assuming colour means albedo
- domain conversion

### Required evidence
- RGBA AOVs
- material response toggle

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-014 — Alpha grille

### Preserve and inspect
- retain source card
- measure pattern and depth context
- choose shot-class representation

### Primary risks
- discarding correct distant LOD
- hero alpha with no thickness

### Required evidence
- grazing angle
- shadow
- distance ladder

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-015 — Alpha decals/badges

### Preserve and inspect
- classify sticker, painted graphic or embossed detail
- preserve mask and UV

### Primary risks
- turning every decal into geometry
- ignoring clearcoat layer order

### Required evidence
- macro edge
- reflection continuity

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-016 — Headlamp alpha/emissive layers

### Preserve and inspect
- separate off-state geometry/material from emissive mask
- identify internal layers

### Primary risks
- uniform emission
- bloom masking missing optics

### Required evidence
- off-state render
- state controller

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-017 — LOD family reconstruction

### Preserve and inspect
- pair objects by name/bounds/material
- compare feature survival
- use lower LOD as topology clue

### Primary risks
- assuming LOD0 is always cleanest
- mixing scales

### Required evidence
- overlay and distance metrics

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-018 — Collision meshes

### Preserve and inspect
- retain as separate class
- use as volume/reference only

### Primary risks
- rendering collision geometry
- using it as final silhouette

### Required evidence

- naming

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-019 — Wheel reconstruction

### Preserve and inspect
- verify diameter/offset/width
- preserve radial symmetry
- separate tyre/brake/hub

### Primary risks
- incorrect origin
- non-circular wheel from scale
- rotational blur artifacts

### Required evidence
- spin test
- dimension check

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-020 — Panel separation

### Preserve and inspect
- keep doors/hood/bumper separate when source provides them
- restore gaps and hinges only as needed

### Primary risks
- joining destroys gaps and material/process IDs

### Required evidence
- gap width
- animation/pivot

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-021 — Retopology patch

### Preserve and inspect
- define trusted boundary and landmarks
- project to source
- compare reflection
- transfer data selectively

### Primary risks
- patch seam
- source faceting copied
- shrinkwrap offset

### Required evidence
- distance/normal/reflection AOV

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-022 — Complete body retopology

### Preserve and inspect
- use only when source cannot meet target
- establish silhouette and reflection landmarks
- retain source as reference

### Primary risks
- all-quads fetish
- loss of exact form

### Required evidence
- held-out camera comparison
- mirror animation

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-023 — UV transfer to rebuilt mesh

### Preserve and inspect
- choose mapping mode by topology/proximity
- limit distance
- inspect seams

### Primary risks
- cross-panel projection
- overlap changes

### Required evidence
- UV checker
- texture diff

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-024 — Normal transfer to rebuilt mesh

### Preserve and inspect
- use clean source or dedicated normal cage
- scope by panel
- preserve sharp edges

### Primary risks
- nearest face on wrong panel
- dependency cycles

### Required evidence
- normal angular error
- moving strip

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-025 — Material-ID recovery

### Preserve and inspect
- use slots, polygons, textures, vertex masks and naming
- record uncertainty

### Primary risks
- merging visually similar materials too early

### Required evidence
- ID colour render

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-026 — Scale recovery

### Preserve and inspect
- use wheelbase, tyre size, badge or glass dimensions
- compare multiple known measurements

### Primary risks
- single unreliable dimension
- unapplied nonuniform scale

### Required evidence
- dimension report

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-027 — Hierarchy and pivots

### Preserve and inspect
- preserve door/wheel/light pivots
- record parent inverse and constraints

### Primary risks
- applying transforms destroys animation logic

### Required evidence
- open/rotate test

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-028 — Hidden/backside geometry

### Preserve and inspect
- inspect normals and occluded surfaces
- keep needed reflections/shadows

### Primary risks
- deleting internal lamp/grille depth
- double shells

### Required evidence
- section view
- ray/reflection test

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-029 — Duplicate/near-duplicate meshes

### Preserve and inspect
- hash topology/positions and compare transforms/materials
- identify instances/LOD/variants

### Primary risks
- deleting model variants
- breaking shared data

### Required evidence
- duplicate report

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


## ABR2-SALV-030 — Texture resolution and mip evidence

### Preserve and inspect
- preserve original files
- record dimensions/format/channels
- inspect alpha and compression

### Primary risks
- upscaling as data recovery
- sRGB on data maps

### Required evidence
- channel preview
- mip/distance test

**Decision output:** `PRESERVE`, `DATA_REPAIR`, `LOCAL_REBUILD`, `RETOPOLOGY`, or `REBUILD` with confidence.


# Part XXXII — Geometry, topology and appearance metrics

## ABR2-METRIC-001 — Distance is necessary but insufficient

Useful distance metrics include:

- point-to-surface distance;
- bidirectional distance;
- percentile Hausdorff distance;
- RMS distance;
- signed distance where orientation permits;
- landmark error.

A low distance does not guarantee:

- good reflection flow;
- correct normals;
- correct silhouette;
- good topology;
- correct UV/tangent behavior.

## ABR2-METRIC-002 — Normal error

For corresponding points:

```text
angle_error = acos(clamp(dot(normalize(N_a), normalize(N_b)), -1, 1))
```

Record:

- mean;
- median;
- 95th/99th percentile;
- maximum;
- spatial heatmap;
- sign/flip count.

## ABR2-METRIC-003 — Reflection-line error

Render or compute line phase under a controlled environment.

Record:

- line displacement;
- kink magnitude;
- curvature of reflected line;
- temporal derivative as light moves;
- panel-boundary discontinuity.

## ABR2-METRIC-004 — Triangle quality for rendering

Useful diagnostics:

- minimum/maximum angle;
- aspect ratio;
- area;
- edge-length ratio;
- sliver count;
- valence;
- sudden density changes.

These are warnings, not universal pass/fail limits. A long thin triangle can be valid if aligned to a controlled feature and not causing interpolation problems.

## ABR2-METRIC-005 — UV quality

Record:

- island area;
- texel density;
- stretch;
- overlap;
- mirrored orientation;
- padding;
- seam length;
- source/target correspondence.

## ABR2-METRIC-006 — Silhouette error

Evaluate multiple cameras, especially:

- front three-quarter;
- rear three-quarter;
- side;
- top;
- low grazing;
- wheel arch;
- lamp corners;
- aero edges.

## ABR2-METRIC-007 — Material scale

For every procedural field record:

- metres/Blender unit;
- feature pitch in mm;
- texture resolution;
- projected pixels at benchmark cameras;
- LOD thresholds.

## ABR2-METRIC-008 — Open-area ratio

For grilles/perforations:

```text
open_area_ratio = open projected area / total projected patch area
```

Preserve this across hero and proxy representations.

## ABR2-METRIC-009 — Weave statistics

Record:

- tow pitch;
- tow width;
- gap width;
- crimp amplitude;
- shear angle;
- orientation;
- seam phase;
- print-through PSD.

## ABR2-METRIC-010 — Metal process statistics

Record:

- scratch orientation distribution;
- groove pitch;
- roughness/normal PSD;
- DOI/edge spread;
- anisotropy;
- defect density.

## ABR2-METRIC-011 — Transparent assembly metrics

Record:

- visible transmittance proxy;
- tint;
- reflected-image contrast;
- interface count;
- thickness;
- haze;
- internal ghosting;
- lamp off/on ratio.

## ABR2-METRIC-012 — Performance

Record per material/part:

- compile time;
- render time;
- VRAM;
- evaluated triangles;
- generated instances;
- texture memory;
- AOV overhead;
- denoiser effect;
- EEVEE/Cycles branch.

---

# Part XXXIII — Deep practical workflows

## ABR2-WORK-001 — Body shell

1. preserve source;
2. verify units and transforms;
3. render mirror/strip diagnostics;
4. classify each panel A–E;
5. preserve custom normals;
6. correct inverted faces only on working copy;
7. repair local topology before global modifiers;
8. use Data Transfer only with scoped mapping;
9. rebuild gaps/edges where silhouettes or reflections require;
10. validate under moving strips;
11. only then apply paint.

## ABR2-WORK-002 — Carbon aero component

1. determine whether part is woven, UD, SMC or painted composite;
2. inspect real seam/ply direction;
3. create physical-scale direction field;
4. assign mould-face/resin/clearcoat stack;
5. add print-through only if reference supports it;
6. build cut edge;
7. add condition;
8. test macro-to-distance LOD.

## ABR2-WORK-003 — Chrome grille surround

1. classify plated metal, plated ABS, PVD, metallisation or polished metal;
2. validate geometry with mirror;
3. establish actual edge radius;
4. build correct stack;
5. add process microtexture;
6. test against black/white cards;
7. add damage revealing the correct substrate.

## ABR2-WORK-004 — Headlamp

1. preserve outer shell and internal objects;
2. separate cover, bezel, reflector, projector, guide and emitters;
3. fix outer-lens thickness/normals;
4. rebuild reflector geometry if faceted;
5. create off-state materials first;
6. add light states;
7. compare hero and baked branches;
8. add bloom last.

## ABR2-WORK-005 — Wheel/brake corner

1. recover wheel centre/diameter/width;
2. separate tyre, wheel finishes, hub, rotor, caliper;
3. rebuild repeated/radial detail;
4. classify wheel process;
5. classify iron versus C/SiC rotor;
6. add wear tied to rotation/pad path;
7. spin-test temporal behavior;
8. validate LOD.

## ABR2-WORK-006 — Alpha grille conversion

1. keep original card;
2. extract pattern and scale;
3. identify expanded/woven/perforated/slat class;
4. generate hero geometry;
5. match open-area ratio;
6. preserve supports and rear depth;
7. bake normal/opacity proxy;
8. transition by shot class.

## ABR2-WORK-007 — Custom-normal body repair

1. save original;
2. record current normals;
3. identify geometry versus normal defect;
4. test imported normals;
5. test recalculated normals;
6. test weighted normals;
7. test transferred normals;
8. compare with true-normal and reflection AOVs;
9. accept the least destructive method.

## ABR2-WORK-008 — Retopology decision

Retopology is justified when:

- silhouette/form is trusted;
- source topology prevents required editing/subdivision;
- shading cannot be repaired without geometry changes;
- target shots expose facets;
- data can be rebaked/transferred.

Retopology is not justified merely because the source is triangulated.

# Part XXXIV — Expanded falsifiable experiment register


## EXP-ABR2-001 — Weave draft truth table

**Category:** Composites

**Hypothesis:** procedural over-under state matches the declared weave draft

### Setup
- render cell IDs and over-under mask for plain, 2×2 and 4×4 patterns
- use a named scene state and deterministic seed;
- save raw EXR, viewable image, diagnostics and manifest;
- record Blender build, engine, samples and device.

### Failure condition
- topology violates draft or changes with scale

### Required decision
- `ACCEPT`, `REVISE`, `REJECT`, or `CONTEXT-DEPENDENT` with evidence.


## EXP-ABR2-002 — Physical tow pitch

**Category:** Composites

**Hypothesis:** millimetre-scale pitch remains constant across objects

### Setup
- apply one material to differently sized panels using shared coordinates
- use a named scene state and deterministic seed;
- save raw EXR, viewable image, diagnostics and manifest;
- record Blender build, engine, samples and device.

### Failure condition
- pitch follows object bounds

### Required decision
- `ACCEPT`, `REVISE`, `REJECT`, or `CONTEXT-DEPENDENT` with evidence.


## EXP-ABR2-003 — Drape shear

**Category:** Composites

**Hypothesis:** direction fields preserve plausible weave shear on compound curvature

### Setup
- compare rigid UV, manual field and simulated drape
- use a named scene state and deterministic seed;
- save raw EXR, viewable image, diagnostics and manifest;
- record Blender build, engine, samples and device.

### Failure condition
- pattern remains planar or folds discontinuously

### Required decision
- `ACCEPT`, `REVISE`, `REJECT`, or `CONTEXT-DEPENDENT` with evidence.


## EXP-ABR2-004 — Crimp ablation

**Category:** Composites

**Hypothesis:** crimp normal contributes identifiable tow highlight structure

### Setup
- render crimp off/low/reference/high
- use a named scene state and deterministic seed;
- save raw EXR, viewable image, diagnostics and manifest;
- record Blender build, engine, samples and device.

### Failure condition
- only colour changes

### Required decision
- `ACCEPT`, `REVISE`, `REJECT`, or `CONTEXT-DEPENDENT` with evidence.


## EXP-ABR2-005 — Resin-richness

**Category:** Composites

**Hypothesis:** resin field changes depth/interface without changing weave identity

- controlled coupon with resin gradient

- save raw EXR, viewable image, diagnostics and manifest;
- record Blender build, engine, samples and device.

### Failure condition

### Required decision
- `ACCEPT`, `REVISE`, `REJECT`, or `CONTEXT-DEPENDENT` with evidence.


## EXP-ABR2-006 — Print-through PSD

**Hypothesis:** low-amplitude ply topography affects reflected strips at correct wavelengths

- band-limited height sweep
- use a named scene state and deterministic seed;
- save raw EXR, viewable image, diagnostics and manifest;
- record Blender build, engine, samples and device.

### Failure condition
- weave becomes embossed fabric

### Required decision
- `ACCEPT`, `REVISE`, `REJECT`, or `CONTEXT-DEPENDENT` with evidence.

## EXP-ABR2-025 — Mould texture spectrum

### Setup

## REF-CG-FABRIC-CAPTURE-2024

- **Type:** research preprint

- **Supports:** explicit fibre/yarn geometry, appearance capture and multiscale conversion

# Legacy v2 manifest snapshot

The following v2 manifest is retained for append-only provenance. The active v3 manifest appears at the end of this document.

# Part XXXVI — Active v2 manifest

```json
{
  "title": "Automotive Body Materials & Geometry R&D Master",
  "version": "2.0",
  "date": "2026-07-15",
  "authoritative_artifact_count": 1,
  "authoritative_artifact": "Automotive_Body_RnD_Master_v2_COMPREHENSIVE_UNIFIED.md",
  "embedded_foundation": "Car Paint Research Compendium v12 preserved verbatim through v1",
  "correction": "v1 was an architecture draft; v2 substantially deepens materials, processes, Blender implementation and game-asset salvage",
  "blender_baseline": "5.0.1",
  "forward_reference": "5.2 LTS",
  "v2_additions": {
    "detailed_material_cards": 40,
    "manufacturing_process_cards": 30,
    "node_and_geometry_nodes_specs": 20,
    "modifier_cards": 19,
    "game_asset_salvage_playbooks": 30,
    "new_falsifiable_experiments": 64,
    "expanded_reference_records": 50
  },
  "evidence_warning": "Most new implementations remain HYP/INFERENCE until executed in Blender 5.0.1. Official documentation and primary research are distinguished from secondary locators.",
  "next_phase": "Run the forensic audit on an actual vehicle asset, then turn the highest-priority material and geometry cards into LIVE-5.0.1 artifacts."
}
```

# v2 conclusion

V2 is no longer merely a body-wide table of contents.

It now contains a substantial practical and research layer for:

- composite topology and manufacturing;
- plated, deposited, machined and aged metals;
- moulded and coated polymers;
- glazing and lamp assemblies;
- wheel, brake, tyre and film systems;
- game-source preservation;
- normal/tangent/UV recovery;
- part-specific reconstruction;
- Blender modifier decisions;
- quantitative validation;
- experiments and source roles.

It remains honest about the next missing evidence:

- live Blender 5.0.1 node and modifier observations;
- target-specific OEM/manufacturer construction data;
- measured samples;
- actual game-model salvage results;
- performance benchmarks.

# Part XXXVII — v3 body-part and source-recovery expansion

## ABR3-CORRECTION-001 — Part-first completeness

V2 deepened material and modifier categories, but a usable automotive-body source also requires explicit part ownership. The following atlas connects each exterior component to plausible materials, assembly geometry, common game-source representations, least-destructive rehabilitation, Blender interventions and validation.

**Part cards in this atlas:** 99

The cards are not claims that every vehicle uses every listed material. They define the candidate space that must be narrowed with OEM, photographic, measured or source-asset evidence.

## ABR3-PART-001-BODY-HOOD — Bonnet or hood outer assembly

### Real material candidates
- painted steel
- painted aluminium
- painted CFRP
- exposed CFRP

### Real assembly and geometry requirements
- large Class-A reflection field
- inner and outer shell
- hemmed edge
- vents, washer nozzles and latch structure

### Common game-source representations and failures
- faceted broad highlight
- zero thickness
- shut line fused into body
- underside deleted

### Least-destructive rehabilitation path
- preserve dense source if true form is good
- rebuild local patches only
- recover shell thickness and latch or vent cut-outs
- preserve the pristine source object and its LOD/material relations;
- classify the result as Preserve, Data Repair, Local Reconstruction, Retopology or Rebuild;
- avoid joining, welding, recalculating normals or applying modifiers before the source-data audit.

### Material-system linkage
- select the material stack from the listed candidates rather than from appearance alone;
- record substrate, manufacturing route, layer stack and exposed condition;
- connect to Paint V12, composite, metal, polymer, glass, lamp, rubber or film modules as appropriate;
- maintain one material identity across hero, production and proxy tiers.

### Suitable Blender intervention candidates
- Data Transfer when trusted UV, custom-normal or mask data can be preserved;
- local Bevel, Solidify, Shrinkwrap, Mirror, Geometry Nodes or Subdivision only when the named geometry defect requires it;
- Weighted Normal or Normal Edit only as scoped shading operations, never as proof of correct form;
- Geometry Nodes for repeatable details, attributes and hero-to-proxy generation.

### Required diagnostics
- perfect-mirror pass;
- neutral dielectric or diffuse isolation pass;
- true-normal and shading-normal comparison;
- silhouette and section views;
- source-versus-repaired overlay;
- UV, tangent and material-ID inspection where textured.

### Part-specific validation
- Mirror diagnostic
- moving strip
- silhouette
- gap section
- open-state clearance

### Acceptance record
- physical dimensions and confidence;
- source object, LOD and material IDs;
- modifier/node settings and order;
- before/after geometry and normal metrics;
- still, motion and performance evidence;
- known limits and rollback path.

**Initial status:** `HYP/INFERENCE`; target-specific evidence and Blender 5.0.1 execution required.

## ABR3-PART-002-BODY-FENDER — Front fender or wing

### Real material candidates
- painted steel
- painted aluminium
- composite

### Real assembly and geometry requirements
- wheel-arch lip
- three-panel junctions
- bumper and hood interfaces
- side marker or vent openings

### Common game-source representations and failures
- arch faceting
- pinched panel junction
- black painted gaps
- mirrored asymmetry

### Least-destructive rehabilitation path
- local retopology around arch
- preserve imported normals
- restore real gaps
- compare left and right before mirroring
- preserve the pristine source object and its LOD/material relations;
- classify the result as Preserve, Data Repair, Local Reconstruction, Retopology or Rebuild;
- avoid joining, welding, recalculating normals or applying modifiers before the source-data audit.

### Material-system linkage
- select the material stack from the listed candidates rather than from appearance alone;
- record substrate, manufacturing route, layer stack and exposed condition;
- connect to Paint V12, composite, metal, polymer, glass, lamp, rubber or film modules as appropriate;
- maintain one material identity across hero, production and proxy tiers.

### Suitable Blender intervention candidates
- Data Transfer when trusted UV, custom-normal or mask data can be preserved;
- local Bevel, Solidify, Shrinkwrap, Mirror, Geometry Nodes or Subdivision only when the named geometry defect requires it;
- Weighted Normal or Normal Edit only as scoped shading operations, never as proof of correct form;
- Geometry Nodes for repeatable details, attributes and hero-to-proxy generation.

### Required diagnostics
- perfect-mirror pass;
- neutral dielectric or diffuse isolation pass;
- true-normal and shading-normal comparison;
- silhouette and section views;
- source-versus-repaired overlay;
- UV, tangent and material-ID inspection where textured.

### Part-specific validation
- Moving strip over arch
- tyre clearance
- front three-quarter silhouette
- normal-deviation AOV

### Acceptance record
- physical dimensions and confidence;
- source object, LOD and material IDs;
- modifier/node settings and order;
- before/after geometry and normal metrics;
- still, motion and performance evidence;
- known limits and rollback path.

**Initial status:** `HYP/INFERENCE`; target-specific evidence and Blender 5.0.1 execution required.

## ABR3-PART-003-BODY-DOOR — Door outer and edge assembly

### Real material candidates
- painted steel
- painted aluminium
- composite skin
- exposed carbon skin

### Real assembly and geometry requirements
- large reflection surface
- handle recess
- window frame
- hemmed edge
- hinge and latch pivots

### Common game-source representations and failures
- flat plane
- wrong pivot
- handle fused to body
- missing edge thickness and inner return

### Least-destructive rehabilitation path
- preserve pivot hierarchy
- reconstruct handle recess and panel edges
- transfer normals only after geometry validation
- preserve the pristine source object and its LOD/material relations;
- classify the result as Preserve, Data Repair, Local Reconstruction, Retopology or Rebuild;
- avoid joining, welding, recalculating normals or applying modifiers before the source-data audit.

## ABR3-PART-005-BODY-ROOF — Roof outer panel

### Real material candidates
- painted steel
- aluminium
- exposed carbon
- glass-roof surround

### Real assembly and geometry requirements
- broad low-curvature crown
- roof ditch
- antenna
- panoramic-roof aperture
- weld or bonded seams

### Common game-source representations and failures
- wavy reflections
- incorrect crown
- carbon weave stretched uniformly
- glass fused into roof

### Least-destructive rehabilitation path
- use cross-sections and reflection lines
- preserve direction field for carbon
- separate glass and seals
- preserve the pristine source object and its LOD/material relations;
- classify the result as Preserve, Data Repair, Local Reconstruction, Retopology or Rebuild;
- avoid joining, welding, recalculating normals or applying modifiers before the source-data audit.

## ABR3-PART-006-BODY-TRUNK — Boot or trunk lid

### Real material candidates
- painted metal
- painted composite
- exposed carbon

### Real assembly and geometry requirements
- large rear reflection
- spoiler mounts
- hinges
- lamp and licence-panel transitions

### Common game-source representations and failures
- edge too thin
- spoiler joined
- bad rear highlight
- incorrect pivot

### Least-destructive rehabilitation path
- rebuild hemmed edge and hinge logic
- separate spoiler
- retain lower shell if visible
- preserve the pristine source object and its LOD/material relations;
- classify the result as Preserve, Data Repair, Local Reconstruction, Retopology or Rebuild;
- avoid joining, welding, recalculating normals or applying modifiers before the source-data audit.

### Material-system linkage

- bows
- rear glass

### Common game-source representations and failures
- plastic shader
- no weave direction
- no tension
- impossible folds

### Least-destructive rehabilitation path
- use cloth-specific material and deformation states
- preserve seam and frame hierarchy
- preserve the pristine source object and its LOD/material relations;
- classify the result as Preserve, Data Repair, Local Reconstruction, Retopology or Rebuild;
- avoid joining, welding, recalculating normals or applying modifiers before the source-data audit.

### Material-system linkage
- select the material stack from the listed candidates rather than from appearance alone;
- record substrate, manufacturing route, layer stack and exposed condition;
- connect to Paint V12, composite, metal, polymer, glass, lamp, rubber or film modules as appropriate;
- maintain one material identity across hero, production and proxy tiers.

### Suitable Blender intervention candidates
- Data Transfer when trusted UV, custom-normal or mask data can be preserved;
- local Bevel, Solidify, Shrinkwrap, Mirror, Geometry Nodes or Subdivision only when the named geometry defect requires it;
- Weighted Normal or Normal Edit only as scoped shading operations, never as proof of correct form;
- Geometry Nodes for repeatable details, attributes and hero-to-proxy generation.

### Required diagnostics
- perfect-mirror pass;
- neutral dielectric or diffuse isolation pass;
- true-normal and shading-normal comparison;
- silhouette and section views;
- source-versus-repaired overlay;
- UV, tangent and material-ID inspection where textured.

### Part-specific validation
- Open and closed animation
- wet and dry states
- grazing cloth response

### Acceptance record
- physical dimensions and confidence;
- source object, LOD and material IDs;
- modifier/node settings and order;
- before/after geometry and normal metrics;
- still, motion and performance evidence;
- known limits and rollback path.

**Initial status:** `HYP/INFERENCE`; target-specific evidence and Blender 5.0.1 execution required.

- Data Transfer when trusted UV, custom-normal or mask data can be preserved;
- local Bevel, Solidify, Shrinkwrap, Mirror, Geometry Nodes or Subdivision only when the named geometry defect requires it;
- Weighted Normal or Normal Edit only as scoped shading operations, never as proof of correct form;
- Geometry Nodes for repeatable details, attributes and hero-to-proxy generation.

### Required diagnostics
- perfect-mirror pass;
- neutral dielectric or diffuse isolation pass;

- silhouette and section views;
- source-versus-repaired overlay;
- UV, tangent and material-ID inspection where textured.

### Part-specific validation
- Front and wheel-well views
- shadow

### Acceptance record
- physical dimensions and confidence;
- source object, LOD and material IDs;
- modifier/node settings and order;
- before/after geometry and normal metrics;
- still, motion and performance evidence;
- known limits and rollback path.

- select the material stack from the listed candidates rather than from appearance alone;
