- Geometry Nodes for repeatable details, attributes and hero-to-proxy generation.

### Required diagnostics
- perfect-mirror pass;
- neutral dielectric or diffuse isolation pass;
- true-normal and shading-normal comparison;
- silhouette and section views;
- source-versus-repaired overlay;
- UV, tangent and material-ID inspection where textured.

### Part-specific validation
- Low angle
- damage

### Acceptance record
- physical dimensions and confidence;
- source object, LOD and material IDs;
- modifier/node settings and order;
- before/after geometry and normal metrics;
- still, motion and performance evidence;
- known limits and rollback path.

**Initial status:** `HYP/INFERENCE`; target-specific evidence and Blender 5.0.1 execution required.

## ABR3-PART-094-UNDER-FELT — Fibre aero liner

### Real material candidates
- needle-punched textile or fibre composite

### Real assembly and geometry requirements
- matte fibrous surface
- moulded shell
- clips and damage

### Common game-source representations and failures
- black plastic

### Least-destructive rehabilitation path
- fibre material and simple solid geometry
- preserve the pristine source object and its LOD/material relations;
- classify the result as Preserve, Data Repair, Local Reconstruction, Retopology or Rebuild;
- avoid joining, welding, recalculating normals or applying modifiers before the source-data audit.

### Material-system linkage
- select the material stack from the listed candidates rather than from appearance alone;
- record substrate, manufacturing route, layer stack and exposed condition;
- connect to Paint V12, composite, metal, polymer, glass, lamp, rubber or film modules as appropriate;
- maintain one material identity across hero, production and proxy tiers.

### Suitable Blender intervention candidates
- Data Transfer when trusted UV, custom-normal or mask data can be preserved;
- local Bevel, Solidify, Shrinkwrap, Mirror, Geometry Nodes or Subdivision only when the named geometry defect requires it;
- Weighted Normal or Normal Edit only as scoped shading operations, never as proof of correct form;
- Geometry Nodes for repeatable details, attributes and hero-to-proxy generation.

### Required diagnostics
- perfect-mirror pass;
- neutral dielectric or diffuse isolation pass;
- true-normal and shading-normal comparison;
- silhouette and section views;
- source-versus-repaired overlay;
- UV, tangent and material-ID inspection where textured.

### Part-specific validation
- Underside
- wet and dry

### Acceptance record
- physical dimensions and confidence;
- source object, LOD and material IDs;
- modifier/node settings and order;
- before/after geometry and normal metrics;
- still, motion and performance evidence;
- known limits and rollback path.

**Initial status:** `HYP/INFERENCE`; target-specific evidence and Blender 5.0.1 execution required.

# Part XXXVIII — Import-format recovery atlas

A format does not determine whether an asset is usable. The exporter, importer, source engine and preservation of normals, tangents, hierarchy and materials are part of the evidence.

## ABR3-FORMAT-01-FBX

**Pipeline role:** Scene-exchange container frequently used for game and DCC assets

### Potentially preserved
- hierarchy
- transforms
- meshes
- material slots
- UV sets
- animation
- skinning
- custom normals depending exporter

### Primary risks
- axis and unit conversion
- split vertices
- negative scale
- embedded-media paths
- exporter-specific material semantics

### Mandatory recovery actions
- record Blender importer options and source exporter
- verify world dimensions
- compare imported and recalculated normals
- preserve pivots
- preserve the untouched source and import settings;
- record object, primitive, material, UV, normal, tangent and animation counts;
- compare a diagnostic render with any available source-engine reference.

**Status:** format-level guidance; exact importer behavior requires Blender 5.0.1 execution.

## ABR3-FORMAT-02-OBJ-MTL

**Pipeline role:** Text geometry format with companion legacy material description

### Potentially preserved
- positions
- UVs
- normals
- groups
- smoothing groups
- material assignments

### Primary risks
- no standard hierarchy or animation
- unit ambiguity
- vendor MTL extensions
- vertex splitting at seams

### Mandatory recovery actions
- preserve groups and normals
- compare smoothing
- reconstruct material channels from source evidence rather than MTL labels alone
- preserve the untouched source and import settings;
- record object, primitive, material, UV, normal, tangent and animation counts;
- compare a diagnostic render with any available source-engine reference.

**Status:** format-level guidance; exact importer behavior requires Blender 5.0.1 execution.

## ABR3-FORMAT-03-GLTF-GLB

**Pipeline role:** Open runtime-oriented transmission format

### Potentially preserved
- scene nodes
- mesh primitives
- normals
- tangents
- UVs
- PBR materials
- textures
- animation and extensions

### Primary risks
- unsupported extensions
- tangent and normal-map parity
- texture transforms
- alpha modes
- compressed mesh or textures

### Mandatory recovery actions
- record extensions
- use Khronos validation where possible
- preserve primitive boundaries and triangulation
- inspect every texture colour space
- preserve the untouched source and import settings;
- record object, primitive, material, UV, normal, tangent and animation counts;
- compare a diagnostic render with any available source-engine reference.

**Status:** format-level guidance; exact importer behavior requires Blender 5.0.1 execution.

## ABR3-FORMAT-04-USD

**Pipeline role:** Layered scene-description ecosystem

### Potentially preserved
- hierarchy
- references
- variants
- instancing
- animation
- materials and metadata depending pipeline

### Primary risks
- composition arcs and variants can hide source choices
- renderer-specific material translation
- unit and up-axis metadata

### Mandatory recovery actions
- record stage layers and selected variants
- preserve instancing
- compare translated materials and transforms
- preserve the untouched source and import settings;
- record object, primitive, material, UV, normal, tangent and animation counts;
- compare a diagnostic render with any available source-engine reference.

**Status:** format-level guidance; exact importer behavior requires Blender 5.0.1 execution.

## ABR3-FORMAT-05-ALEMBIC

**Pipeline role:** Baked geometry and transform cache

### Potentially preserved
- animated vertices
- topology over time
- UV and other attributes depending exporter

### Primary risks
- limited editable semantics
- material reconstruction may be separate
- topology can vary per frame

### Mandatory recovery actions
- record frame range and topology variance
- inventory attributes
- retain source scene if available
- preserve the untouched source and import settings;
- record object, primitive, material, UV, normal, tangent and animation counts;
- compare a diagnostic render with any available source-engine reference.

**Status:** format-level guidance; exact importer behavior requires Blender 5.0.1 execution.

## ABR3-FORMAT-06-PLY

**Pipeline role:** Polygon or point-cloud interchange

### Potentially preserved
- positions
- normals
- colours and custom properties depending implementation

### Primary risks
- usually no standard PBR material
- unit ambiguity
- property names vary

### Mandatory recovery actions
- inventory header properties
- verify scale, normals and colour domain
- classify scan versus final mesh
- preserve the untouched source and import settings;
- record object, primitive, material, UV, normal, tangent and animation counts;
- compare a diagnostic render with any available source-engine reference.

**Status:** format-level guidance; exact importer behavior requires Blender 5.0.1 execution.

## ABR3-FORMAT-07-STL

**Pipeline role:** Tessellated geometry exchange commonly originating from CAD

### Potentially preserved
- triangle positions and facet normals

### Primary risks
- no UVs, materials, hierarchy or reliable units
- often contains dense CAD tessellation

### Mandatory recovery actions
- recover scale from real dimensions
- preserve surface before any remesh
- decide whether dense tessellation is already the best form source
- preserve the untouched source and import settings;
- record object, primitive, material, UV, normal, tangent and animation counts;
- compare a diagnostic render with any available source-engine reference.

**Status:** format-level guidance; exact importer behavior requires Blender 5.0.1 execution.

## ABR3-FORMAT-08-DAE-COLLADA

**Pipeline role:** XML scene and asset exchange

### Potentially preserved
- hierarchy
- geometry
- materials
- animation and controllers depending exporter

### Primary risks
- inconsistent exporter support
- axis and material translation
- duplicate nodes

### Mandatory recovery actions
- record importer behavior
- compare object counts, transforms and materials with source
- preserve the untouched source and import settings;
- record object, primitive, material, UV, normal, tangent and animation counts;
- compare a diagnostic render with any available source-engine reference.

**Status:** format-level guidance; exact importer behavior requires Blender 5.0.1 execution.

## ABR3-FORMAT-09-GAME-PROPRIETARY

**Pipeline role:** Engine-specific extracted mesh and material package

### Potentially preserved
- potentially exact runtime data including LODs, packed maps, custom normals, bones and shader parameters

### Primary risks
- unknown channel packing
- lossy extractor
- undocumented coordinate and tangent conventions
- legal restrictions

### Mandatory recovery actions
- record extractor and plugin versions
- preserve raw dumps
- compare with original game captures
- do not redistribute without permission
- preserve the untouched source and import settings;
- record object, primitive, material, UV, normal, tangent and animation counts;
- compare a diagnostic render with any available source-engine reference.

**Status:** format-level guidance; exact importer behavior requires Blender 5.0.1 execution.

# Part XXXIX — Texture-channel archaeology

Texture names are clues, not definitions. Every channel must be isolated, interpreted in the source shader context and assigned an explicit colour space.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-01-BASE-COLOR — Base Color or albedo

**Possible meaning:** surface or body colour under the source renderer's definition

**Initial colour-space treatment:** usually sRGB colour data

### Interpretation risks
- may contain baked AO, lighting, cavity, tint or paint effect
- metallic workflow semantics differ

### Mandatory checks
- solo under neutral lighting
- inspect source shader
- compare with AO removed
- record gamut and alpha
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-02-DIFFUSE-LEGACY — Legacy diffuse map

**Possible meaning:** older diffuse contribution rather than guaranteed modern Base Color

**Initial colour-space treatment:** usually sRGB

### Interpretation risks
- may include baked specular exclusion or lighting
- not automatically Principled Base Color

### Mandatory checks
- reconstruct source shading first
- compare to game reference
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-03-ROUGHNESS — Roughness map

**Possible meaning:** microfacet spread parameter or authored proxy

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- may be perceptually remapped, packed or inverted
- renderer distribution differs

### Mandatory checks
- inspect numeric range
- solo grayscale
- test known values in source-like shader
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-04-GLOSSINESS — Gloss or smoothness map

**Possible meaning:** often inverse-like smoothness control

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- not universally exactly one minus roughness
- gamma or curve may differ

### Mandatory checks
- A/B conversion against source captures
- record conversion
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-05-METALLIC — Metallic map

**Possible meaning:** metalness workflow classification or blend

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- effect-pigment paint does not imply whole surface is metallic
- anti-aliased edges produce mixed values

### Mandatory checks
- cross-check material IDs and source shader
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-06-SPECULAR-LEVEL — Specular scalar

**Possible meaning:** dielectric F0, legacy highlight intensity or proprietary parameter

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- cannot be mapped to IOR without semantics

### Mandatory checks
- inspect source documentation and exemplar materials
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-07-SPECULAR-COLOR — Specular colour

**Possible meaning:** conductor colour or legacy coloured highlight

**Initial colour-space treatment:** sRGB or linear depending pipeline

### Interpretation risks
- may encode metal reflectance or artistic tint

### Mandatory checks
- classify conductor versus dielectric
- test neutral body colour
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-08-NORMAL — Normal map

**Possible meaning:** tangent-space or object-space normal perturbation

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- green-channel convention, tangent basis, mirrored UVs and triangulation are critical

### Mandatory checks
- test Y-positive and Y-negative
- freeze triangulation
- rotate light
- inspect seams
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-09-DETAIL-NORMAL — Detail normal

**Possible meaning:** secondary high-frequency normal field

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- requires correct normal composition and UV scale
- may be engine tiled

### Mandatory checks
- compare RNM, height-first and engine-like blend
- record scale
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-10-HEIGHT — Height or displacement

**Possible meaning:** scalar surface field for displacement, parallax or bump

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- unknown midpoint, sign and physical scale
- may be authored for parallax only

### Mandatory checks
- inspect range
- test midpoint
- calibrate against geometry
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-11-AMBIENT-OCCLUSION — Ambient occlusion

**Possible meaning:** local visibility approximation

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- often packed
- destructive albedo multiplication creates dirt-like results

### Mandatory checks
- solo output
- trace source multiplication
- route by lobe if retained
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-12-CAVITY — Cavity mask

**Possible meaning:** small-scale concavity or edge mask

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- not equivalent to AO, curvature or dirt

### Mandatory checks
- compare against geometry and normal
- inspect scale
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-13-CURVATURE — Curvature or edge-wear mask

**Possible meaning:** convexity or concavity proxy

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- may be baked in object space and fail after topology changes

### Mandatory checks
- compare with rebuilt geometry
- rebake if needed
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-14-OPACITY — Opacity or coverage

**Possible meaning:** cutout, dither, blend weight or transmission mask

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- premultiplication
- cutout versus transparent material
- alpha may hold another channel

### Mandatory checks
- test over contrasting backgrounds
- inspect alpha mode
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-15-EMISSION — Emission colour or mask

**Possible meaning:** self-luminous term, colour or activation mask

**Initial colour-space treatment:** sRGB for colour, Non-Color for mask

### Interpretation risks
- may require separate engine colour and intensity and state

### Mandatory checks
- render off and on states
- separate material emission from bloom
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-16-CLEARCOAT — Clearcoat mask

**Possible meaning:** outer coat weight or material selector

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- engine-specific semantics
- may identify a separate material instead

### Mandatory checks
- coat-only diagnostic
- inspect source shader
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-17-CLEARCOAT-ROUGHNESS — Clearcoat roughness or gloss

**Possible meaning:** outer-interface roughness

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- may be inverted or remapped

### Mandatory checks
- compare coat highlight only
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-18-TRANSMISSION — Transmission weight

**Possible meaning:** through-surface scattering or refraction control

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- alpha is not transmission
- thin and solid semantics differ

### Mandatory checks
- compare solid and thin glass
- inspect source material flags
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-19-THICKNESS — Thickness map

**Possible meaning:** volume path length or subsurface or transmission proxy

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- units, zero and scale are unknown

### Mandatory checks
- compare against section geometry and known thickness
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-20-MATERIAL-ID — Material or palette ID

**Possible meaning:** discrete material selection encoded in colour or integer values

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- anti-aliased boundaries
- palette interpretation
- compression can corrupt IDs

### Mandatory checks
- nearest sampling
- colour-ID preview
- map values to source materials
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-21-MASK-PACK — Packed mask texture

**Possible meaning:** several scalar channels such as ORM, RMA or custom masks

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- channel order varies
- alpha may be independent
- names are unreliable

### Mandatory checks
- solo R, G, B and A
- correlate with source shader
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-22-VERTEX-COLOR — Vertex or corner colour attributes

**Possible meaning:** tint, AO, mask, material ID, wind, damage or engine-specific data

**Initial colour-space treatment:** depends on use

### Interpretation risks
- domain and interpolation matter
- colour does not imply albedo

### Mandatory checks
- render every channel as AOV
- trace source shader and domain
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-23-LIGHTMAP — Lightmap or baked illumination

**Possible meaning:** precomputed static lighting

**Initial colour-space treatment:** usually colour data but pipeline-specific

### Interpretation risks
- should not be folded into reusable material
- may encode directionality or HDR

### Mandatory checks
- separate from intrinsic material
- use only when recreating source-game lighting
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-24-DECAL-MASK — Decal or overlay mask

**Possible meaning:** graphic, badge, dirt, damage or material transition

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- layer order and UV set may differ
- may rely on deferred decal system

### Mandatory checks
- identify owning surface and blend mode
- inspect alternate UV
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

## ABR3-CHANNEL-25-FLOW-MAP — Flow or direction map

**Possible meaning:** 2D vector field for anisotropy, water, carbon direction or effect motion

**Initial colour-space treatment:** Non-Color

### Interpretation risks
- vector remapping and coordinate basis may be proprietary

### Mandatory checks
- decode channel range
- render direction hue
- test object rotation
- inspect the original bit depth, compression, alpha and dimensions;
- render the channel alone through an AOV or emission diagnostic;
- document every inversion, remap, curve and blend used during conversion.

### Acceptance boundary
- the converted Blender material must reproduce the source channel's intended role, not merely look plausible in one lighting setup.

# Part XL — Procedural and reconstruction algorithms

## ABR3-ALG-001 — Plain-weave crossing topology

Let the physically scaled weave coordinates be `u` and `v`.

```text
i = floor(u)
j = floor(v)
fu = fract(u)
fv = fract(v)
warp_over = parity(i + j) == 0
warp_distance = abs(fu - 0.5)
weft_distance = abs(fv - 0.5)
```

The parity defines only which tow is above at a crossing.

Separate controls still own:

- tow width;
- gap width;
- tow profile;
- crimp amplitude;
- fibre microstructure;
- resin depth;
- clearcoat.

Hard binary height changes are prohibited. Smooth profiles must preserve the declared over-under state while avoiding discontinuous shading normals.

**Blender route:** Math nodes, Vector Math, Separate XYZ, Fraction, Floor, Modulo/Compare, Smoothstep or Color Ramp, then a single height-to-normal conversion per physical layer.

**Diagnostic outputs:** integer cell ID, warp mask, weft mask, over-under state, crimp height, pitch in millimetres.

## ABR3-ALG-002 — 2×2 twill draft

A simple topological draft can use:

```text
phase = mod(i - j, 4)
warp_over = phase == 0 or phase == 1
```

The sign and axis convention must match the reference orientation.

Variation belongs in:

- tow path;
- width;
- drape shear;
- resin;
- local deformation;
- print-through.

Variation must not randomly violate the weave draft.

## ABR3-ALG-003 — 4×4 twill draft

A simple eight-step progression can be expressed as:

```text
phase = mod(i - j, 8)
warp_over = phase in {0, 1, 2, 3}
```

This creates four-over/four-under topology. It is not enough by itself to reproduce a real 4×4 twill because tow profile, nesting, local shear and material response remain separate.

## ABR3-ALG-004 — Tow profile and crimp

Use a smooth cross-sectional profile rather than a square strip.

Candidate normalized tow profile:

```text
q = clamp(distance_from_tow_centre / half_width, 0, 1)
profile = (1 - q^p)^r
```

Parameters `p` and `r` control shoulder and crown shape.

At a crossing, modulate the upper and lower tow heights with a smooth crossing envelope. The crimp wavelength follows the weave draft; it is not generic noise.

## ABR3-ALG-005 — Drape-direction field

Store two tangent-plane directions:

```text
D_warp_raw
D_weft_raw
```

Project them onto the selected surface normal:

```text
D_warp = normalize(D_warp_raw - N * dot(N, D_warp_raw))
D_weft = normalize(D_weft_raw - N * dot(N, D_weft_raw))
```

Do not force the two directions perpendicular if the cloth has sheared.

For complex panels, a globally integrated or authored parameterization is preferable to reconstructing coordinates from local dot products alone.

**Geometry Nodes route:** guide curves, transferred vector attributes, UV-derived direction, drape simulation bake or manually painted vector map.

## ABR3-ALG-006 — Drape-shear metric

```text
shear_angle = acos(clamp(dot(D_warp, D_weft), -1, 1))
deviation_from_90 = shear_angle - π/2
```

Use this as:

- a diagnostic;
- a possible tow-width/gap modifier;
- a wrinkle-risk indicator.

Do not make shear visible as arbitrary colour unless the physical material effect requires it.

## ABR3-ALG-007 — Forged-carbon bundle mask

Use a point or Voronoi feature only as a stable bundle centre and random seed.

For each bundle:

```text
local = P - centre
x = dot(local, direction)
y = dot(local, perpendicular)
ellipse = (x / half_length)^2 + (y / half_width)^2
bundle_mask = smoothstep(1 + feather, 1 - feather, ellipse)
```

Add low-amplitude local-coordinate distortion to avoid perfect ellipses.

A plausible field layers:

- short bundles;
- medium bundles;
- long bundles;
- buried/dim bundles;
- surface/resin-rich bundles.

Bundle ID, orientation, length, width and depth remain independent outputs.

## ABR3-ALG-008 — Compression-flow orientation

A mould-flow direction field may be supplied by:

- manually authored curves;
- geodesic distance from charge locations;
- approximate vector diffusion;
- external process simulation;
- measured orientation map.

Bundle azimuth may be sampled around the local flow direction with a declared angular spread.

The result should show coherent but imperfect flow, not a perfectly combed surface.

## ABR3-ALG-009 — Fibre microstructure

Within each tow or bundle, build fine variation primarily along the fibre direction.

Useful components:

- filament-scale bands;
- tow-width intensity variation;
- resin interruptions;
- low-amplitude tangent-normal variation.

Gabor or authored directional textures can generate microstructure, but the field must inherit the tow direction and be filtered before it aliases.

## ABR3-ALG-010 — Linear brushed-metal frame

Given surface normal `N` and process direction `T_raw`:

```text
T = normalize(T_raw - N * dot(N, T_raw))
B = normalize(cross(N, T))
```

Coordinates:

```text
along = dot(P, T)
across = dot(P, B)
```

Build the surface from:

- fine abrasion mostly varying across the brush;
- medium stroke bundles;
- sparse deep scratches;
- cross-scratches;
- polishing haze.

One Wave texture is not an adequate brushed-metal model.

## ABR3-ALG-011 — Radial machining frame

Given centre `C`, axis `A` and surface point `P`:

```text
R = P - C
R_plane = R - A * dot(A, R)
radius = length(R_plane)
radial = normalize(R_plane)
tangent = normalize(cross(A, radial))
```

Handle the centre singularity with a mask or fallback.

Tool grooves may be functions of radius, helical phase or measured tool path. The coordinate system must rotate with the wheel.

## ABR3-ALG-012 — Heat-affected oxide field

Build a process-history scalar from:

- distance to weld;
- distance along exhaust path;
- source temperature proxy;
- material class;
- exposure time;
- cooling and shielding masks.

Map that scalar to oxide-film thickness, then evaluate a thin-film or fitted colour response.

Soot, scratches and base-metal roughness remain separate.

## ABR3-ALG-013 — Mould-surface spectrum

Represent mould texture as a sum of physical bands:

```text
H(P) = Σ A_k * H_k(P / λ_k)
```

For each band store:

- wavelength `λ_k` in millimetres;
- amplitude `A_k`;
- anisotropy;
- dominant direction;
- skewness and kurtosis target;
- manufacturing source.

Equal RMS height does not imply equal reflected appearance.

## ABR3-ALG-014 — Flow and weld-line proxy

For two or more approximate polymer flow fronts:

1. compute distance or travel time from each gate/source;
2. find regions where two arrival fields are nearly equal;
3. form a narrow weld-front mask;
4. orient local texture using the contributing flow directions;
5. use the mask only to modulate physically plausible surface or colour properties.

The weld line is not generic dirt.

## ABR3-ALG-015 — Panel-gap construction

A hero panel gap contains:

- outer edge radius of panel A;
- outer edge radius of panel B;
- physical gap width;
- recess depth;
- inner wall or seal;
- material continuity and paint termination.

At distant LOD, preserve:

- average darkness;
- gap width;
- edge highlight;
- panel separation.

A black texture line is only a proxy and must be labelled as such.

## ABR3-ALG-016 — Reference-distance field

Measure both directions where possible:

```text
d_candidate_to_reference
d_reference_to_candidate
```

Record:

- mean;
- RMS;
- median;
- 95th and 99th percentile;
- maximum;
- spatial heatmap.

A low distance score does not guarantee correct reflection or normals.

## ABR3-ALG-017 — Normal angular error

For normalized normals:

```text
c = clamp(dot(N_reference, N_candidate), -1, 1)
angle_error = acos(c)
```

Record both:

- angle magnitude;
- local tangent-frame direction of the error.

Two equal angular errors can deflect highlights in opposite directions.

## ABR3-ALG-018 — Reflection-line comparison

Use one fixed stripe environment or procedural reflected-line field.

Compare:

- line phase;
- lateral displacement;
- kink angle;
- curvature;
- temporal derivative while the stripe moves;
- discontinuity across panel boundaries.

This better reflects automotive-surface quality than vertex count alone.

## ABR3-ALG-019 — Open-area conservation

For a grille or perforated proxy:

```text
open_area_ratio = projected_open_area / projected_total_area
```

Match this ratio across:

- hero geometry;
- medium proxy geometry;
- opacity card.

Also compare silhouette, directional reflection and shadowing. Matching open area alone is not full equivalence.

## ABR3-ALG-020 — Alpha-to-geometry reconstruction

1. preserve the original alpha texture and card;
2. measure pattern pitch, orientation and boundary;
3. identify expanded, woven, perforated, honeycomb or custom pattern;
4. reconstruct parametric hero geometry;
5. fit projected mask and open area;
6. add thickness, bevel and supporting frame;
7. create baked normal and opacity proxies;
8. test hero-to-proxy transition in motion.

## ABR3-ALG-021 — Light-guide extraction proxy

A simplified causal model:

```text
remaining(s + ds) = remaining(s) * exp(-absorption * ds)
emitted(s) = remaining(s) * extraction_density(s)
```

This is not full total-internal-reflection ray tracing. It is preferable to uniform emission because source position, path loss and extraction density remain explicit.

## ABR3-ALG-022 — Tyre contact patch

For a static or slowly moving hero tyre:

- flatten the tread near the road;
- bulge the sidewall;
- compress blocks and sipes;
- approximately preserve volume;
- avoid intersection;
- preserve wheel centre and rolling radius as required by the shot.

Use a shape key, lattice or controlled Geometry Nodes deformation. A perfectly circular loaded tyre undermines vehicle weight.

## ABR3-ALG-023 — Brake active-track mask

Given rotor radius `r`:

```text
active = smooth_annulus(r_inner, r_outer)
```

Use:

- pad sweep;
- usage state;
- wet/storage history;
- temperature;
- pad-transfer field.

Fresh rust primarily occurs outside the active swept region or after inactivity.

## ABR3-ALG-024 — Representation-conserving LOD

Across geometry, normal-map, opacity and homogeneous tiers preserve the quantities relevant to the part:

- silhouette;
- mean reflectance or transmittance;
- orientation;
- normal variance;
- pattern identity;
- open-area ratio;
- emitted flux;
- material separation.

Use overlapping smooth weights. Avoid hard swaps inside visible shot ranges.

## ABR3-ALG-025 — Texture-channel confidence

For every recovered texture channel, store:

```text
semantic_candidate
source_shader_evidence
colour_space
conversion
confidence
contradictions
```

No automatic filename heuristic should silently become production truth.

## ABR3-ALG-026 — Modifier ablation

For a proposed modifier stack:

1. render source;
2. add one modifier;
3. record geometry, normal, UV and performance changes;
4. render diagnostics;
5. continue only if the previous step is understood;
6. test selected order permutations where interactions are expected.

A good final result with an unexplained stack is not a reproducible solution.

# Part XLI — Geometry, material and pipeline failure library

Every failure card links a visible symptom to a likely domain, diagnostic evidence and a corrective direction. It is not permission to apply the correction without verifying the cause.

## ABR3-FAIL-01-BODY-FACETING — Broad paint highlight breaks into polygons

**Likely domain:** geometric tessellation, shading normals or both

### Inspect
- perfect mirror
- true-normal AOV
- imported and recalculated normals
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- preserve the source if form is good
- repair normals
- retopologise only if geometry remains faceted

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-02-SUBD-PINCH — Subdivision pinches around poles or triangles

**Likely domain:** control topology and extraordinary-vertex placement

### Inspect
- limit-surface strip
- crease and support-loop ablation
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- move topology away from highlight-critical areas or preserve the dense source

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-03-SUBD-SWELL — Subdivision body drifts or swells from the reference

**Likely domain:** insufficient control cage or wrong boundary behavior

### Inspect
- distance heatmap
- silhouette overlay
- section curves
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- add form controls or abandon the SubD conversion

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-04-WEIGHTED-WAVE — Weighted Normal creates moving waviness

**Likely domain:** custom normals no longer correspond well to actual geometry

### Inspect
- modifier on and off
- object rotation
- true-normal comparison
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- scope or remove the modifier
- correct geometry or transfer better normals

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-05-NORMAL-SEAM — Shading seam appears across a UV or material boundary

**Likely domain:** split normals, duplicate vertices, tangent mismatch or map seam

### Inspect
- normal map off
- custom normals off
- UV and sharp-edge overlay
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- identify the data boundary before welding or recalculating

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-06-NORMAL-Y — Dents become bumps or shading is inverted

**Likely domain:** normal-map green-channel convention mismatch

### Inspect
- Y-positive and Y-negative comparison
- known test normal
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- document and apply the correct convention

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-07-TRI-DIAGONAL — Normal-map shading changes after quad conversion or triangulation

**Likely domain:** different diagonal changes tangent interpolation

### Inspect
- original and alternate triangulation
- seam render
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- freeze the source-compatible triangulation

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-08-NEGATIVE-SCALE — Mirrored side has twisted or inverted shading

**Likely domain:** negative transform determinant and tangent handedness

### Inspect
- world-normal AOV
- applied-transform duplicate
- text orientation
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- correct transforms on a copy and recompute or rebake carefully

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-09-UV-CARBON-MIRROR — Carbon weave reverses or phase-jumps

**Likely domain:** mirrored UV islands or wrong direction field

### Inspect
- UV orientation, warp and weft AOVs
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- use unique mapping or side-specific vector fields where visible

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-10-GENERATED-SCALE — Procedural feature size differs between objects

**Likely domain:** Generated coordinates normalised to object bounds

### Inspect
- millimetre checker
- shared Object coordinate
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- use shared rig coordinates or stored physical position

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-11-CHROME-DULL — Chrome appears flat grey

**Likely domain:** environment, geometry, roughness or metal classification is wrong

### Inspect
- black and white reflection cards
- perfect mirror baseline
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- fix environment and geometry before brightening colour

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-12-CHROME-TOY — Chrome looks like liquid toy plastic

**Likely domain:** wrong edge radius, perfect smoothness or dielectric material

### Inspect
- radius ladder
- conductor check
- process-texture ablation
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- classify the real stack and add correct geometry and microfinish

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-13-CARBON-CHECKER — Carbon looks like a printed checkerboard

**Likely domain:** missing tow depth, crimp, resin and drape

### Inspect
- topology, resin and coat ablation
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- build real weave ownership and direction fields

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-14-CARBON-GLITTER — Carbon sparkles like metallic paint

**Likely domain:** metallicity or white-speckle misuse

### Inspect
- fibre and resin AOVs
- compact-light test
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- remove fake flakes and use directional fibre response

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-15-FORGED-GRANITE — Forged carbon looks like granite or stone

**Likely domain:** raw Voronoi cells shown directly

### Inspect
- cell-ID and bundle-mask AOVs
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- use elongated overlapping bundles with depth and orientation

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-16-PLASTIC-CHALK — Black trim appears uniformly dusty or grey

**Likely domain:** UV-age mask used as a global rough white overlay

### Inspect
- fresh and aged reference states
- body-colour and roughness AOVs
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- localise exposure and alter bulk, microtexture and wear coherently

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-17-PIANO-FLAT — Piano black lacks depth and reflected image

**Likely domain:** missing clear interface or poor environment and geometry

### Inspect
- coat on and off
- strip reflection
- black substrate AOV
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- build black substrate, high-gloss coat and separate condition layers

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-18-GLASS-BLACK — Windows read as opaque black

**Likely domain:** tint too strong, no interior illumination or wrong transparency settings

### Inspect
- unclamped transmission
- interior card
- side view
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- calibrate absorption and engine mode
- restore physical thickness

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-19-GLASS-GHOST — Glass produces excessive duplicate reflections

**Likely domain:** coincident shells, nested interfaces or wrong normals

### Inspect
- section view
- interface toggles
- face-normal display
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

### Corrective direction
- clean the solid stack and remove coincident faces

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-20-LAMP-GLOW-CARD — Lamp realism depends entirely on bloom

- bloom off
- emission AOV
- off-state beauty
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

- rebuild lens, guide, reflector and housing before post effects

- preserve and compare the untouched source;

- resolution, distance and motion ladder
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

- tread and sidewall solo passes
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

- contact-patch closeup
- shadow and deformation pass
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

- flatten tread, bulge sidewall and integrate road contact

## ABR3-FAIL-33-WELD-SEAM-LOSS — UV, material or hard-normal seam disappears after cleanup

- seam and vertex counts before and after
- texture check
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

## ABR3-FAIL-34-TRANSFORM-BREAK — Animation or pivots fail after applying transforms

**Likely domain:** parent inverse, constraints or local axes changed

- open, fold and spin states before and after
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;

## ABR3-FAIL-35-ALPHA-HERO — Alpha mesh looks acceptable front-on but fails at grazing angles

- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

- before and after stills;

## ABR3-FAIL-36-DETAIL-OVERSCALE — Procedural scratches, weave or mould texture looks miniature or gigantic

- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;

- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;

## ABR3-FAIL-39-LOD-POP — Material or geometry changes identity during camera motion

**Likely domain:** hard threshold or non-conserving representations

- LOD-weight AOV and slow pullback
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;

## ABR3-FAIL-40-MATERIAL-MERGE — Different black or silver parts collapse into one generic material

**Likely domain:** appearance-based naming replaced process classification

- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;

## ABR3-FAIL-41-REFERENCE-OVERFIT — Asset matches one photograph but fails elsewhere

**Likely domain:** lighting, camera or post effects were baked into material decisions

### Inspect
- held-out lights and views
- preserve and compare the untouched source;
- determine whether the symptom belongs to geometry, normals, UV/tangent data, material, sampling or post-processing.

- fit only intrinsic material and geometry
- keep camera/post separate

### Required evidence
- before and after stills;
- the relevant AOV, mesh overlay or section view;
- motion test if the symptom can crawl, swim or pop;
- rollback path and decision record.

# Part XLII — Part-by-part qualification experiment register

**Experiments generated from body-part atlas:** 99

Every part experiment must compare the untouched source, the rehabilitated candidate and any proxy or LOD representation. A part is not accepted because its isolated beauty render looks good.

## EXP-ABR3-001 — Bonnet or hood outer assembly qualification

**Part ID:** `ABR3-PART-001-BODY-HOOD`

### Hypothesis
The rehabilitated bonnet or hood outer assembly preserves its real assembly logic, plausible material identity and reflection or silhouette behavior better than the untouched runtime representation without introducing unjustified geometry or material complexity.

### Candidate material classes
- painted steel
- painted aluminium
- painted CFRP
- exposed CFRP

### Geometry claims under test
- large Class-A reflection field
- inner and outer shell
- hemmed edge
- vents, washer nozzles and latch structure

### Source failures this experiment must discriminate
- faceted broad highlight
- zero thickness
- shut line fused into body
- underside deleted

### Candidate intervention under test
- preserve dense source if true form is good
- rebuild local patches only
- recover shell thickness and latch or vent cut-outs

### Standard render matrix
- source mesh with imported data;
- source mesh with neutral mirror material;
- repaired candidate with neutral mirror material;
- repaired candidate with final classified material;
- proxy or lower-LOD candidate where applicable;
- one broad strip, one narrow strip, diffuse environment and representative automotive studio;
- front, side, grazing and macro camera where physically possible;
- slow camera or object motion if repeated, reflective, transparent or emissive detail is involved.

### Diagnostics
- source and candidate silhouette overlay;
- world-space position or nearest-reference distance heatmap;
- true normal, shading normal and normal-deviation AOV;
- material-ID and repair-class AOV;
- UV/tangent diagnostic for textured parts;
- physical-scale checker for repeated detail;
- geometry, texture and shader memory/performance report.

### Part-specific checks
- Mirror diagnostic
- moving strip
- silhouette
- gap section
- open-state clearance

### Reject or revise when
- the repair improves one camera but introduces worse reflection flow elsewhere;
- material identity depends on one HDRI or post effect;
- modifiers hide unexplained geometry errors;
- repeated detail crawls, swims or changes scale;
- a fresh file cannot reproduce the result;
- the source asset contains useful data that the repair destroyed;
- the complexity increase has no measurable visual or production benefit.

### Decision values
- `PRESERVE_SOURCE`;
- `DATA_REPAIR_ACCEPTED`;
- `LOCAL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `RETOPOLOGY_ACCEPTED`;
- `FULL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `PROXY_ONLY`;
- `REJECTED`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`.

- repaired candidate with neutral mirror material;

- proxy or lower-LOD candidate where applicable;
- one broad strip, one narrow strip, diffuse environment and representative automotive studio;
- front, side, grazing and macro camera where physically possible;
- slow camera or object motion if repeated, reflective, transparent or emissive detail is involved.

### Diagnostics
- source and candidate silhouette overlay;
- world-space position or nearest-reference distance heatmap;
- true normal, shading normal and normal-deviation AOV;
- material-ID and repair-class AOV;
- UV/tangent diagnostic for textured parts;
- physical-scale checker for repeated detail;
- geometry, texture and shader memory/performance report.

### Part-specific checks
- Full opening
- glass-body reflection
- seal compression
- rear silhouette

### Reject or revise when
- the repair improves one camera but introduces worse reflection flow elsewhere;
- material identity depends on one HDRI or post effect;
- modifiers hide unexplained geometry errors;
- repeated detail crawls, swims or changes scale;
- a fresh file cannot reproduce the result;
- the source asset contains useful data that the repair destroyed;
- the complexity increase has no measurable visual or production benefit.

### Decision values
- `PRESERVE_SOURCE`;
- `DATA_REPAIR_ACCEPTED`;
- `LOCAL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `RETOPOLOGY_ACCEPTED`;
- `FULL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `PROXY_ONLY`;
- `REJECTED`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`.

## EXP-ABR3-020 — Convertible textile roof qualification

**Part ID:** `ABR3-PART-020-BODY-SOFTTOP`

### Hypothesis
The rehabilitated convertible textile roof preserves its real assembly logic, plausible material identity and reflection or silhouette behavior better than the untouched runtime representation without introducing unjustified geometry or material complexity.

### Candidate material classes
- woven textile
- rubber backing
- glass window
- frame and seals

### Geometry claims under test
- cloth tension
- stitched seams
- fold pattern
- bows
- rear glass

### Source failures this experiment must discriminate
- plastic shader
- no weave direction
- no tension
- impossible folds

### Candidate intervention under test
- use cloth-specific material and deformation states
- preserve seam and frame hierarchy

### Standard render matrix
- source mesh with imported data;
- source mesh with neutral mirror material;
- repaired candidate with neutral mirror material;
- repaired candidate with final classified material;
- proxy or lower-LOD candidate where applicable;
- one broad strip, one narrow strip, diffuse environment and representative automotive studio;
- front, side, grazing and macro camera where physically possible;
- slow camera or object motion if repeated, reflective, transparent or emissive detail is involved.

### Diagnostics
- source and candidate silhouette overlay;
- world-space position or nearest-reference distance heatmap;
- true normal, shading normal and normal-deviation AOV;
- material-ID and repair-class AOV;
- UV/tangent diagnostic for textured parts;
- physical-scale checker for repeated detail;
- geometry, texture and shader memory/performance report.

### Part-specific checks
- Open and closed animation
- wet and dry states
- grazing cloth response

### Reject or revise when
- the repair improves one camera but introduces worse reflection flow elsewhere;
- material identity depends on one HDRI or post effect;
- modifiers hide unexplained geometry errors;
- repeated detail crawls, swims or changes scale;
- a fresh file cannot reproduce the result;
- the source asset contains useful data that the repair destroyed;
- the complexity increase has no measurable visual or production benefit.

### Decision values
- `PRESERVE_SOURCE`;
- `DATA_REPAIR_ACCEPTED`;
- `LOCAL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `RETOPOLOGY_ACCEPTED`;
- `FULL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `PROXY_ONLY`;
- `REJECTED`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`.

## EXP-ABR3-033 — Radiator or condenser visible through grille qualification

**Part ID:** `ABR3-PART-033-OPEN-RADIATOR`

### Hypothesis
The rehabilitated radiator or condenser visible through grille preserves its real assembly logic, plausible material identity and reflection or silhouette behavior better than the untouched runtime representation without introducing unjustified geometry or material complexity.

### Candidate material classes
- aluminium fins and tubes
- dark coatings

### Geometry claims under test
- deep repeated fins
- support frame
- bug and dirt exposure

### Source failures this experiment must discriminate
- single grey plane
- oversized normal map

### Candidate intervention under test
- hero patch plus filtered proxy
- preserve depth and darkness physically

### Standard render matrix
- source mesh with imported data;
- source mesh with neutral mirror material;
- repaired candidate with neutral mirror material;
- repaired candidate with final classified material;
- proxy or lower-LOD candidate where applicable;
- one broad strip, one narrow strip, diffuse environment and representative automotive studio;
- front, side, grazing and macro camera where physically possible;
- slow camera or object motion if repeated, reflective, transparent or emissive detail is involved.

### Diagnostics
- source and candidate silhouette overlay;
- world-space position or nearest-reference distance heatmap;
- true normal, shading normal and normal-deviation AOV;
- material-ID and repair-class AOV;
- UV/tangent diagnostic for textured parts;
- physical-scale checker for repeated detail;
- geometry, texture and shader memory/performance report.

### Part-specific checks
- Grille-through view
- moiré
- exposure

### Reject or revise when
- the repair improves one camera but introduces worse reflection flow elsewhere;
- material identity depends on one HDRI or post effect;
- modifiers hide unexplained geometry errors;
- repeated detail crawls, swims or changes scale;
- a fresh file cannot reproduce the result;
- the source asset contains useful data that the repair destroyed;
- the complexity increase has no measurable visual or production benefit.

### Decision values
- `PRESERVE_SOURCE`;
- `DATA_REPAIR_ACCEPTED`;
- `LOCAL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `RETOPOLOGY_ACCEPTED`;
- `FULL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `PROXY_ONLY`;
- `REJECTED`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`.

## EXP-ABR3-046 — Projector module qualification

**Part ID:** `ABR3-PART-046-LAMP-PROJECTOR`

### Hypothesis
The rehabilitated projector module preserves its real assembly logic, plausible material identity and reflection or silhouette behavior better than the untouched runtime representation without introducing unjustified geometry or material complexity.

### Candidate material classes
- glass or polymer lens
- metal reflector
- cutoff shield

### Geometry claims under test
- deep assembly
- lens curvature
- source
- bezel and shield

### Source failures this experiment must discriminate
- uniform glowing disc
- missing internals

### Candidate intervention under test
- build lens, shield, reflector and source
- separate off and on states

### Standard render matrix
- source mesh with imported data;
- source mesh with neutral mirror material;
- repaired candidate with neutral mirror material;
- repaired candidate with final classified material;
- proxy or lower-LOD candidate where applicable;
- one broad strip, one narrow strip, diffuse environment and representative automotive studio;
- front, side, grazing and macro camera where physically possible;
- slow camera or object motion if repeated, reflective, transparent or emissive detail is involved.

### Diagnostics
- source and candidate silhouette overlay;
- world-space position or nearest-reference distance heatmap;
- true normal, shading normal and normal-deviation AOV;
- material-ID and repair-class AOV;
- UV/tangent diagnostic for textured parts;
- physical-scale checker for repeated detail;
- geometry, texture and shader memory/performance report.

### Part-specific checks
- Off and on
- side view
- cutoff proxy

### Reject or revise when
- the repair improves one camera but introduces worse reflection flow elsewhere;
- material identity depends on one HDRI or post effect;
- modifiers hide unexplained geometry errors;
- repeated detail crawls, swims or changes scale;
- a fresh file cannot reproduce the result;
- the source asset contains useful data that the repair destroyed;
- the complexity increase has no measurable visual or production benefit.

### Decision values
- `PRESERVE_SOURCE`;
- `DATA_REPAIR_ACCEPTED`;
- `LOCAL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `RETOPOLOGY_ACCEPTED`;
- `FULL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `PROXY_ONLY`;
- `REJECTED`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`.

- front, side, grazing and macro camera where physically possible;
- slow camera or object motion if repeated, reflective, transparent or emissive detail is involved.

### Diagnostics
- source and candidate silhouette overlay;
- world-space position or nearest-reference distance heatmap;
- true normal, shading normal and normal-deviation AOV;
- material-ID and repair-class AOV;
- UV/tangent diagnostic for textured parts;
- physical-scale checker for repeated detail;
- geometry, texture and shader memory/performance report.

### Part-specific checks
- Long strip
- joint macro

### Reject or revise when
- the repair improves one camera but introduces worse reflection flow elsewhere;
- material identity depends on one HDRI or post effect;
- modifiers hide unexplained geometry errors;
- repeated detail crawls, swims or changes scale;
- a fresh file cannot reproduce the result;
- the source asset contains useful data that the repair destroyed;
- the complexity increase has no measurable visual or production benefit.

### Decision values
- `PRESERVE_SOURCE`;
- `DATA_REPAIR_ACCEPTED`;
- `LOCAL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `RETOPOLOGY_ACCEPTED`;
- `FULL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `PROXY_ONLY`;
- `REJECTED`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`.

- repaired candidate with final classified material;
- proxy or lower-LOD candidate where applicable;
- one broad strip, one narrow strip, diffuse environment and representative automotive studio;
- front, side, grazing and macro camera where physically possible;
- slow camera or object motion if repeated, reflective, transparent or emissive detail is involved.

### Diagnostics
- source and candidate silhouette overlay;
- world-space position or nearest-reference distance heatmap;
- true normal, shading normal and normal-deviation AOV;
- material-ID and repair-class AOV;
- UV/tangent diagnostic for textured parts;
- physical-scale checker for repeated detail;
- geometry, texture and shader memory/performance report.

### Part-specific checks
- Spin
- contact patch
- wet state

### Reject or revise when
- the repair improves one camera but introduces worse reflection flow elsewhere;
- material identity depends on one HDRI or post effect;
- modifiers hide unexplained geometry errors;
- repeated detail crawls, swims or changes scale;
- a fresh file cannot reproduce the result;
- the source asset contains useful data that the repair destroyed;
- the complexity increase has no measurable visual or production benefit.

### Decision values
- `PRESERVE_SOURCE`;
- `DATA_REPAIR_ACCEPTED`;
- `LOCAL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `RETOPOLOGY_ACCEPTED`;
- `FULL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `PROXY_ONLY`;
- `REJECTED`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`.

- `LOCAL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `RETOPOLOGY_ACCEPTED`;
- `FULL_REBUILD_ACCEPTED`;
- `PROXY_ONLY`;
- `REJECTED`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`.

# Part XLIII — Production acceptance system

## ABR3-ACCEPT-001 — Geometry grades

### G0 — Source only

Imported and preserved, not yet evaluated.

### G1 — Audited

Units, hierarchy, topology, normals, UVs, materials and LOD relations recorded.

### G2 — Diagnostic pass

Silhouette, mirror, strip-light and normal diagnostics completed.

### G3 — Rehabilitated

Declared source defects corrected with a rollback path.

### G4 — Shot-qualified

Passes the intended camera, motion, material and resolution range.

### G5 — Reusable

Clean hierarchy, documented modifiers, stable attributes, no hidden dependencies.

### G6 — Reproduced

A fresh Blender 5.0.1 environment recreates the result.

## ABR3-ACCEPT-002 — Material grades

### M0 — Unclassified

Material is identified only by game name or appearance.

### M1 — Family classified

Substrate and broad process family are plausible.

### M2 — Stack specified

Layer order, surface process and directional fields are explicit.

### M3 — Implemented

Shader/geometry exists with diagnostics.

### M4 — Controlled validation

Passes appropriate light, view and distance tests.

### M5 — Target-qualified

Matches a selected OEM, measured or photographic target within declared limits.

### M6 — Production reusable

Quality tiers preserve identity and performance fits the project.

## ABR3-ACCEPT-003 — Source-confidence grades

- `S0`: guessed from appearance;
- `S1`: game naming or texture clue;
- `S2`: consistent multi-source photographic evidence;
- `S3`: official OEM or supplier disclosure;
- `S4`: measured or physically inspected sample;
- `S5`: target-specific authoritative build/manufacturing data.

A high visual grade does not increase source confidence automatically.

# Part XLIV — Practical checklists

## ABR3-CHECK-IMPORT

- [ ] raw source preserved;
- [ ] checksum recorded;
- [ ] licence and provenance recorded;
- [ ] importer and extractor versions recorded;
- [ ] axis, handedness and scale recorded;
- [ ] object, mesh and material counts recorded;
- [ ] hierarchy, pivots and animation preserved;
- [ ] all UV, colour and generic attributes inventoried;
- [ ] custom-normal and tangent state recorded;
- [ ] source textures copied or linked non-destructively;
- [ ] LOD, collision, helper, alpha and decal classes tagged;
- [ ] original game/reference screenshots archived.

## ABR3-CHECK-GEOMETRY

- [ ] mirror pass;
- [ ] neutral dielectric pass;
- [ ] silhouette cameras;
- [ ] moving strip;
- [ ] true and shading normals;
- [ ] non-manifold and boundary inventory;
- [ ] negative-scale report;
- [ ] loose and duplicate geometry report;
- [ ] panel-gap section views;
- [ ] glass, lamp, grille and thin-part thickness;
- [ ] wheel centres, diameters and pivots;
- [ ] open-state clearances.

## ABR3-CHECK-MATERIAL

- [ ] substrate recorded;
- [ ] manufacturing process recorded;
- [ ] layer stack recorded;
- [ ] physical scale recorded;
- [ ] orientation or process direction recorded;
- [ ] geometry-dependent and shader-dependent features separated;
- [ ] wear, damage and contamination assigned to physical layers;
- [ ] EEVEE and Cycles scope declared;
- [ ] no destructive AO into albedo;
- [ ] no fake metallicity or emission used to imitate unrelated effects;
- [ ] diagnostic AOVs created;
- [ ] source claims and uncertainty documented.

## ABR3-CHECK-MODIFIER

- [ ] defect being solved named;
- [ ] valid source data at risk named;
- [ ] exact settings and order recorded;
- [ ] before and after topology counts;
- [ ] before and after normal metrics;
- [ ] silhouette comparison;
- [ ] moving-strip comparison;
- [ ] UV and material-boundary comparison;
- [ ] dependency and fresh-file test;
- [ ] rollback path.

## ABR3-CHECK-LOD

- [ ] hero representation;
- [ ] production representation;
- [ ] distant proxy;
- [ ] switch or blend range;
- [ ] projected-size metric;
- [ ] silhouette conservation;
- [ ] material mean and orientation conservation;
- [ ] open-area conservation for perforated parts;
- [ ] emission conservation for lamps;
- [ ] motion stress test.

## ABR3-CHECK-ACCEPTANCE

- [ ] scope and target shot class stated;
- [ ] hypothesis and failure condition stated;
- [ ] source, repaired and proxy comparison;
- [ ] still validation;
- [ ] motion validation where relevant;
- [ ] performance budget;
- [ ] fresh-file reproduction;
- [ ] asset metadata and version;
- [ ] accepted, revised or rejected decision;
- [ ] unresolved contradictions retained.
