# Part LIV — ADAS, camera, LiDAR and radar material truth

This part prevents the body master from being visible-light-only. Sensor apertures, paints and contamination now carry wavelength- and system-specific evidence.


## ABR4-SENSOR-001-VISIBLE-NIR — Visible and NIR dual appearance

### Scope
- A surface can match human-visible colour while returning different LiDAR intensity.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Pigments, binders, flakes and substrate have wavelength-dependent response.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Maintain visible material and sensor-response metadata as separate but linked channels.

### Blender implementation route
- NIR albedo/BRDF proxy, wavelength tag and angle dependence.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Visible/NIR split render; intensity-angle curve.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Inferring LiDAR response from RGB luminance.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-LIDAR-PAINT-2023] https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.01346

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.

## ABR4-SENSOR-004-RADAR-RADOME — Radar radome or emblem

### Scope
- RF-transparent stack in front of radar.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Thickness, dielectric properties, paint, metallisation and water films affect 77–81 GHz transmission.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Mark a protected sensor aperture with material restrictions.

### Blender implementation route
- GN/material metadata for radome zone; no conductor layers unless verified.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Dry/wet/ice, angle and polarisation response.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Chrome emblem placed over radar without special construction.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

- [REF-ABR4-LIDAR-REVIEW-2020] https://arxiv.org/abs/2004.08467

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.

## ABR4-SENSOR-012-RETRO-LIDAR — Retroreflective materials in LiDAR scenes

- Licence plates and reflectors can return strong light toward the source.

- Retroreflection depends on source/view separation and microstructure.

- Use dedicated retroreflective approximation, not emission.

# Part LV — Homologation, markings and passive optical devices

This part covers small but high-value evidence that distinguishes a manufactured vehicle from a generic render. It also prevents passive reflectors and approval marks from being implemented as decorative guesses.


## ABR4-REG-001-GLASS-MARK — Safety-glazing approval and maker marks

### Scope
- Etched/printed markings identify glazing type, manufacturer and approval.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Mark location, typography and content are vehicle- and market-specific.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Use target photographs or official part data; treat as decal/etch at correct layer.

### Blender implementation route
- UV/decal or shallow geometry; LOD by shot.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Macro legibility; left/right and market variant.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Invented E-marks or copied marks from another car.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-UNECE-R43] https://unece.org/transport/vehicle-regulations-wp29/standards/addenda-1958-agreement-regulations-41-60

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-REG-002-LAMP-MARK — Lamp approval and moulded markings

### Scope
- Lenses/housings carry approval codes, material marks and optical identifiers.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Often moulded into inner or outer surfaces and distorted by lens curvature.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Capture exact target marking and surface ownership.

### Blender implementation route
- Emboss/deboss geometry or normal map; correct transparent material integration.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Off-state macro; refraction.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Flat white text floating on lens.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-UNECE-R148] https://unece.org/transport/vehicle-regulations-wp29/standards/addenda-1958-agreement-regulations-141-160
- [REF-ABR4-UNECE-R149] https://unece.org/transport/vehicle-regulations-wp29/standards/addenda-1958-agreement-regulations-141-160

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-REG-003-RETROREFLECTOR — Passive retroreflector identity

### Scope
- A reflector returns incident light toward its source and emits no light.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Colour, area, position and microprism/corner-cube structure are regulated or designed.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Build off-state coloured polymer and source-linked retroreflection.

### Blender implementation route
- Normal/geometry microstructure plus angular response proxy.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Coaxial flash and off-axis test.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Emission used for passive reflector.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;

## ABR4-REG-004-LIGHT-POSITION — Lighting-device position and visible area

### Scope
- Lamp placement and orientation are part of vehicle identity and compliance.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Mesh repair must not shift optical reference axes or visible apertures.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Small positional edits can alter both the body fit and the optical reference geometry, so lamp placement must remain datum-controlled.

### Blender implementation route
- Store datum transforms and lens visible area.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Frustum/axis overlays; body-fit and animation states.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Lamp moved for visual symmetry after import.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-UNECE-R48] https://unece.org/transport/vehicle-regulations-wp29/standards/addenda-1958-agreement-regulations-41-60

## ABR4-REG-012-HOMOLOGATION-LOD — Marking LOD and legibility

### Scope
- Tiny legal and manufacturing markings should not shimmer or bloat.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Representation must shift from geometry to normal/texture to omission by projected size.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Maintain correct average shading and silhouette without fake readability.

### Blender implementation route
- Projected-pixel threshold and smooth LOD.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Slow pullback; TAA/denoiser.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Oversized marks to keep them readable.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-CWF-2024] https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.15661

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


# Part LVI — Scan, CAD, game and photographic data fusion

This part formalises how incompatible sources are combined without destroying provenance. It makes uncertainty and source ownership part of the mesh rather than a paragraph in chat history.


## ABR4-FUSION-001-SOURCE-HIERARCHY — Multi-source truth hierarchy

### Scope
- Combine game mesh, lower LOD, scan, CAD, photographs, drawings and real dimensions without pretending they agree.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Each source has different accuracy, completeness and legal status.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Use source-specific confidence and spatial ownership.

### Blender implementation route
- Store source ID per point/part and conflict records; never overwrite provenance.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Conflict heatmap; source coverage; held-out photo.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Averaging all sources into an untraceable mesh.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-MVPS-2020] https://arxiv.org/abs/2001.06659
- [REF-ABR4-GLTF] https://github.com/KhronosGroup/glTF/blob/main/specification/2.0/Specification.adoc

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-FUSION-002-GAME-CAD-FUSION — Game mesh plus CAD-like reference

### Scope
- Runtime mesh may contain styling and UV data while a dense tessellated source carries better form.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Data Transfer can move UV/normals/masks after geometry comparison.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Maintain independent source and candidate collections.

### Blender implementation route
- Registration, local reconstruction and controlled data transfer.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Distance, normal and reflection-line comparison.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Joining both meshes and deleting duplicates blindly.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-BL-DATATRANSFER] https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/5.0/modeling/modifiers/modify/data_transfer.html

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-FUSION-003-SCAN-GAME-FUSION — Scan plus game asset

### Scope
- Scan supplies shape evidence; game asset supplies clean segmentation, UVs, hierarchy and animation.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Specular surfaces and occlusion create scan holes.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Use scan as reference, not automatic final topology.

### Blender implementation route
- Rigid registration, local confidence map, retopo/patch and selective transfer.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Scan residual; silhouette; texture projection.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Shrinkwrapping entire game mesh to noisy scan.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-STRUCTURED-LIGHT] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Structured_light

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-FUSION-004-PHOTOGRAMMETRY — Photogrammetric body reference

### Scope
- Multi-view images recover geometry and camera poses.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Gloss, reflections, repeated textures and glass cause failure.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Useful for dimensions and coarse form with controlled matte/reference targets.

### Blender implementation route
- Calibrated image set, masks, scale bars and lens model.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Reprojection error; scale; reflection artefact mask.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Treating reflected environment as surface texture.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-MVPS-2020] https://arxiv.org/abs/2001.06659

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-FUSION-005-SPECULAR-SCAN — Scanning glossy and transparent surfaces

### Scope
- Ordinary structured light may fail due to reflection, refraction and low texture.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Temporary matte spray or specialised patterns change the capture condition.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Record every treatment and never treat coated scan colour as real material.

### Blender implementation route
- Scan geometry branch, treatment mask and post-cleaning verification.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Repeated scans; untreated control; edge coverage.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Baking scanning spray into final albedo.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-STRUCTURED-LIGHT] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Structured_light

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-FUSION-006-ICP-REGISTRATION — Rigid registration and ICP

### Scope
- Align sources after establishing scale and initial correspondences.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- ICP can converge to a wrong local minimum on symmetric car bodies.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Use landmarks, symmetry-aware constraints and held-out validation.

### Blender implementation route
- Datum landmarks, point-to-plane ICP and outlier rejection.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Landmark residual; left/right ambiguity; overlay.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Pressing align and accepting a low numeric residual.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-MVPS-2020] https://arxiv.org/abs/2001.06659

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-FUSION-007-NONRIGID-REG — Non-rigid registration

### Scope
- Align flexible bumper, tyre, seal or scan state to a nominal reference.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Deformation can be real assembly state, not capture error.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Separate rigid datum alignment from elastic correction.

### Blender implementation route
- Cage/lattice or deformation graph with regularisation and attachment constraints.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Displacement field; strain; gap/flush.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Warping hard body panels freely to match one photo.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

## ABR4-FUSION-010-SYMMETRY — Symmetry with explicit exceptions

### Scope
- Vehicle bodies are broadly symmetric but contain real asymmetries.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Mirroring can improve weak data but erase fuel doors, sensors, exhaust, wipers and market differences.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Maintain asymmetry list and side-specific attributes.

### Blender implementation route
- Mirror on reference branch, restore exceptions and compare sides.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Asymmetry checklist; left/right source confidence.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Mirroring final asset without audit.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

## ABR4-FUSION-011-HOLE-FILL — Scan-hole and occlusion filling

### Scope
- Missing scan data is not proof of a smooth surface.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Use adjacent form, symmetry, CAD/game source and design lines.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Mark reconstructed regions and confidence.

### Blender implementation route
- Patch surfaces with boundary/curvature constraints; retain source gap.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;

# Part LVII — Spectral, colour, polarisation and camera-reference governance

This part keeps material research scene-referred and separates intrinsic appearance from camera, display and environment effects.


## ABR4-COLOR-001-RAW-LINEAR — RAW linearisation and camera profile

### Scope
- Convert sensor data into a known scene-referred state before fitting materials.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Demosaicing, black level, white balance and camera profile change colour and highlights.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Store the full conversion path.

### Blender implementation route
- RAW plus DNG/ICC or measured camera profile; no hidden auto tone.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Grey patch linearity; exposure scaling.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Sampling sRGB screenshots as spectral truth.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-MVPS-2020] https://arxiv.org/abs/2001.06659

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-COLOR-002-ILLUMINANT — Illuminant spectral power distribution

### Scope
- Two lights with equal RGB appearance can produce different material colours.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Metamerism affects paint, plastics, films and tinted glass.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Record illuminant or use measured/standard sources for capture and fitting.

### Blender implementation route
- Multi-illuminant capture and spectral metadata.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Delta E and angular response.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Matching under one LED and assuming daylight parity.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-COATING-REVIEW] https://www.mdpi.com/2079-6412/6/2/24

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.

- Effect paints, carbon, anodised metal and films vary with illumination/view.

## ABR4-COLOR-004-SPECTRAL — Spectral reflectance and transmittance

### Scope
- RGB is a compressed representation of wavelength-dependent behavior.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Important for metamerism, tinted glass, sensor response and thin films.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Store spectra or fitted bases when available; otherwise mark RGB limitation.

### Blender implementation route
- Spectrophotometer/goniometer data and wavelength range.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;

## ABR4-COLOR-005-POLARIZATION — Polarisation state capture

### Scope
- Clearcoat, fibres, glass, water and metals interact with polarisation differently.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Useful for separation and sensor realism, but Blender beauty shaders may not model full polarisation.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Store capture state and use as evidence for layer ownership.

### Blender implementation route
- Parallel/cross/unpolarised triplet and angle.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Degree-of-polarisation proxy.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Claiming a standard RGB shader reproduces polarisation.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-COLOR-006-HIGHLIGHT-CLIP — Highlight clipping control

### Scope
- Chrome, glass, lights and clearcoat require unclipped radiance evidence.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Display clipping and camera saturation erase roughness and intensity information.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Preserve HDR and show false-colour clipping.

### Blender implementation route
- Bracket and raw linear values.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

- Clipping percentage; exposure-invariance.

## ABR4-COLOR-007-LENS-EFFECTS — Lens flare, ghosting and bloom separation

### Scope
- Optical artefacts belong to camera/post, not body material.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Bright lamps and chrome can produce flare independent of surface BRDF.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Create raw material pass and camera-effects pass.

### Blender implementation route
- Lens model, aperture, coating proxy and compositor state.

- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

## ABR4-COLOR-014-UNCERTAINTY-COLOR — Colour and appearance uncertainty

- Sample variation, instrument drift, camera profile and illuminant uncertainty must remain visible.

- Fit ranges and confidence, not one magical RGB value.

- Material parameters should carry confidence and permissible ranges so later fitting does not mistake measurement noise for physical detail.

# Part LVIII — Thermal, mechanical and assembly-state systems

This part adds time, load and assembly state to geometry and materials. It prevents repair decisions from mistaking a dynamic or preloaded state for incorrect modelling.


## ABR4-STATE-001-BRAKE-HEAT — Brake thermal state

### Scope
- Rotor, caliper, wheel and surrounding air change with braking history.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Heat affects oxide colour, dust adhesion, haze and convection.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Drive a thermal field from use state; separate iron and C/SiC responses.

### Blender implementation route
- Temperature AOV, rotor annulus, caliper lag and cool-down.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Cold/hot/cooling sequence.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Rainbow heat map painted on every rotor.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-DRIVAER-2024] https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.11969

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.

- Pipe, tip, shield and nearby bumper experience different temperatures.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Oxide, soot and thermal expansion depend on source and material.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Use flow/path and joint-aware thermal fields.

### Blender implementation route
- Temperature, oxide thickness and soot AOV.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.

# Part LIX — Repair, refinish, replacement and aftermarket states

This part explains why real cars often contradict factory-perfect references. Repair state is now explicit in geometry, coating stack, fit and ageing.


## ABR4-REPAIR-001-REPAINT — Panel repaint and refinish

### Scope
- Replacement colour and clear layers can differ from factory paint.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Blend, texture, film thickness and sanding history create local mismatch.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Use Paint V12 with refinish process ID and blend zone.

### Blender implementation route
- Panel/repair masks, multi-angle match and edge evidence.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Body under several illuminants.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- One constant hue shift painted inside panel boundary.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;

# Part LX — OEM accent, package and factory-option research methodology

This part generalises the paint-option work to carbon packages, wheels, black optics, glazing, brightwork, roofs, brakes and regional hardware without pretending marketing names are material science.


## ABR4-OEM-001-CONFIGURATOR — Official configurator capture

### Scope
- Record model-year and market-specific exterior options from official configurators.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Configurators may use CGI, compressed colour and dynamic lighting; they prove option availability better than exact material values.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Store page date, region, model, option code/name, screenshots and limitations.

### Blender implementation route
- Create option record linked to material candidate, not direct RGB.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Cross-check brochure and press images.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Sampling configurator pixel as factory spectral truth.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-OPENPBR] https://academysoftwarefoundation.github.io/OpenPBR/

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.

- Names and availability vary by market and model year.

### Blender implementation route
- Material stack candidates and source evidence.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Macro photos and damage clues.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Calling every satin silver aluminium.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-COATING-REVIEW] https://www.mdpi.com/2079-6412/6/2/24

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-OEM-010-ROOF-OPTION — Roof and upper-body options

### Scope
- Painted contrast roof, carbon roof, panoramic glass and wrapped roof have distinct stacks and geometry.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Option can alter seals, antenna, roof rails and glass.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- The official option name establishes configuration evidence, while physical material identity remains a separate claim to verify.

### Blender implementation route
- Map package to body and assembly cards.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Cross-section and option dependencies.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Recolouring roof without changing glass/seals.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-GLTF] https://github.com/KhronosGroup/glTF/blob/main/specification/2.0/Specification.adoc

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-OEM-011-REGIONAL — Regional and market variation

### Scope
- Lighting, plates, reflectors, glazing, trim and option names can differ.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- A globally generic asset may combine incompatible features.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Store market as a first-class variant dimension.

### Blender implementation route
- Variant collection and rules.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Market-specific reference set.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Mixing US side markers with EU lamp/plate setup accidentally.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-UNECE-R48] https://unece.org/transport/vehicle-regulations-wp29/standards/addenda-1958-agreement-regulations-41-60

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-OEM-012-MODEL-YEAR — Model-year and running change

### Scope
- Facelifts and supplier changes alter lamps, grilles, finishes and markings.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Build date matters more than generic model name.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Record production range and supersession.

### Blender implementation route
- Part version graph.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Compare official and real references by date.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Averaging pre- and post-facelift details.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-GLTF] https://github.com/KhronosGroup/glTF/blob/main/specification/2.0/Specification.adoc

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-OEM-013-SPECIAL-SERIES — Special series and bespoke programmes

### Scope
- Low-volume options may use hand-applied materials, unique weaves, badges and finish combinations.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Documentation can be incomplete and customer-specific.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Use vehicle-specific evidence and preserve uncertainty.

### Blender implementation route
- Bespoke option record with affected parts.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Several independent photographs.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Generalising one bespoke car to the model range.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-MVPS-2020] https://arxiv.org/abs/2001.06659

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


## ABR4-OEM-014-SOURCE-RANK — OEM option source ranking

### Scope
- Official configurator, brochure, parts data, press, dealer listing, owner photo and forum each support different claims.

### Physical or manufacturing basis
- Availability and physical construction require different evidence.

### Consequence for automotive CG
- Assign source role and confidence per claim.

### Blender implementation route
- Conflict table and date.
- preserve physical scale, coordinate space, owner part and state;
- expose intermediate attributes/AOVs instead of hiding all logic in the final shader;
- create hero, production and proxy representations only after the hero behavior is understood.

### Diagnostics and evidence
- Audit contradictory names/codes.
- compare against untouched source and at least one held-out light/view/state;
- record Blender version, engine, camera, lighting, colour management and parameter hash.

### Failure signatures
- Using low-quality listings to override official documentation.
- the result works only under one HDRI or camera;
- uncertainty or source conflict is silently converted into a precise parameter.

### References
- [REF-ABR4-GLTF] https://github.com/KhronosGroup/glTF/blob/main/specification/2.0/Specification.adoc

**Initial evidence state:** `HYP/INFERENCE` unless the cited source or an embedded earlier section establishes a higher level. `LIVE-5.0.1` requires execution.


# Part LXI — Cross-domain linkage and implementation priority

## ABR4-LINK-001 — Reference capture connects to every material family

The capture system is not a separate documentation exercise.

It supplies:

- physical scale and multiband topography to Paint V12, carbon, moulded plastic, brushed metal and glass;
- parallel/cross-polarised evidence for coat, resin, fibre, scratch and contamination ownership;
- held-out lights and views for every node experiment;
- NIR and RF metadata for sensor-facing materials;
- uncertainty and provenance for repair decisions.

A material card without a capture or source-confidence path remains a hypothesis.

## ABR4-LINK-002 — Class-A diagnostics govern geometry rehabilitation

Reflection lines, gap, flush, edge radius and weak-feature preservation decide whether:

- imported custom normals should be retained;
- Weighted Normal is a valid scoped aid;
- a dense tessellated body should remain intact;
- local reconstruction is sufficient;
- a SubD cage is justified;
- a lower LOD contains useful form clues.

The modifier atlas therefore remains subordinate to the Class-A and source-confidence evidence.

## ABR4-LINK-003 — Joining explains fit, gaps and hidden geometry

Adhesives, sealers, tapes and foams connect:

- glazing position to windscreen bead and frit;
- composite panels to metal mounts and galvanic isolation;
- bumper and lamp fit to clips, seals and assembly preload;
- badges and trim to backing gaps and edge lift;
- water ingress to seam-sealer and bond failure;
- repair state to replacement-part fit.

A black recess cannot replace this assembly logic.

## ABR4-LINK-004 — Condition is a state graph, not a grunge slider

Water, road film, salt, brake dust, insects, UV, corrosion, polishing and repair interact in sequence.

Example:

```text
clean clearcoat
→ road film changes wetting
→ rain redistributes contamination
→ mineral residue remains after evaporation
→ washing creates micro-marring
→ polishing reduces scratches and clear thickness
→ edge burn-through exposes lower layers
→ moisture/salt enables corrosion at the breach
```

Each transition must preserve layer ownership and time.

## ABR4-LINK-005 — Sensor truth branches from the same physical asset

The visible beauty material remains the shared base, while research outputs add:

- NIR reflectance or fitted LiDAR intensity;
- radome-zone material restrictions;
- sensor-window transmittance and contamination;
- passive retroreflection;
- camera obscuration;
- heater and cleaning state.

A separate sensor branch must not silently change visible geometry or material identity.

## ABR4-LINK-006 — OEM packages map to body-part and material records

An official option name is linked through:

```text
model year + market + option code
→ affected body-part IDs
→ actual substrate/process candidates
→ target-specific material stack
→ markings and regional hardware
→ reference set and confidence
```

Marketing labels such as “Black Optics,” “Carbon Package” or “Satin Aluminium” are not shader names.

## ABR4-LINK-007 — Multi-source fusion must preserve conflict

Game mesh, scan, CAD-like tessellation, official photography and dimensions may disagree.

The master requires:

- source IDs on geometry and materials;
- spatial confidence;
- a conflict table;
- untouched references;
- repair-class decisions;
- held-out validation.

The final mesh is not allowed to erase the history of how a region was inferred.

## ABR4-LINK-008 — Recommended first implementation order

1. `ABR4_CAPTURE_MANIFEST`
2. `ABR4_REFLECTION_LINES`
3. `ABR4_SCAN_CONFIDENCE`
4. `ABR4_GAP_FLUSH`
5. `ABR4_MULTI_SOURCE_FUSION`
6. `ABR4_ADHESIVE_BEAD` and glazing section test
7. `ABR4_STATE_CONTROLLER`
8. `ABR4_WETTING_STATE` and `ABR4_WIPER_SWEEP`
9. `ABR4_CORROSION_STACK`
10. `ABR4_SENSOR_VISIBLE_NIR` and `ABR4_RADOME_ZONE`
11. `ABR4_OEM_OPTION_GRAPH`
12. hero-to-production LOD and fresh-file regression

This order establishes evidence and geometry truth before adding complex condition or sensor behavior.

# Part LXII — v4 node and Geometry Nodes implementation cards


## ABR4-NODE-001 — `ABR4_CAPTURE_MANIFEST`

**Domain:** Geometry Nodes/Python metadata

### Inputs
- camera, light, sample and source IDs

### Outputs
- named attributes/JSON manifest

### Purpose
- capture provenance and confidence

### Required implementation discipline
- declare coordinate space and physical units;
- store stable IDs and named diagnostics where repeated features are generated;
- separate geometry ownership, surface-layer ownership and camera/sensor effects;
- provide bypass and ablation controls;
- avoid version-specific socket assumptions until checked in Blender 5.0.1.

### Known boundary
- metadata is not visual truth

**Status:** `HYP`; exact node types, socket names, domains and engine behavior require Blender 5.0.1 validation.


## ABR4-NODE-002 — `ABR4_REFLECTION_LINES`

**Domain:** Shader diagnostic

### Inputs
- surface normal, view vector, stripe axis and phase

### Purpose
- line continuity and temporal kink

### Required implementation discipline
- declare coordinate space and physical units;
- store stable IDs and named diagnostics where repeated features are generated;
- separate geometry ownership, surface-layer ownership and camera/sensor effects;
- provide bypass and ablation controls;
- avoid version-specific socket assumptions until checked in Blender 5.0.1.

- must not be used as final material

**Status:** `HYP`; exact node types, socket names, domains and engine behavior require Blender 5.0.1 validation.

# Part LXIII — v4 falsifiable experiment register


## EXP-ABR4-001 — Linear RAW photographic reference

**Category:** Reference acquisition and metrology

### Hypothesis
- Implementing linear raw photographic reference with the stated physical ownership and state will outperform a generic appearance-only approximation under held-out conditions.

### Controlled setup
- target: A repeatable camera reference pipeline for paint, composite, metal, plastic, glass and contamination.
- implementation branch: Store camera model, lens, focal length, aperture, shutter, ISO, focus distance, RAW converter, profile, white balance, exposure compensation and crop. Preserve unclipped RAW and rendered preview.
- baseline A: untouched source or current production asset;
- baseline B: simplest plausible generic approximation;
- candidate: v4 physically/process-linked implementation;
- fixed camera, light, colour management and deterministic seed;
- raw and denoised outputs where path tracing is used.

### Measurements
- Grey/white/black targets; highlight-clipping map; EXIF manifest; repeat capture with exposure bracket.
- still and motion comparison;
- identity across hero/production/proxy tiers;
- performance and memory;
- source confidence and uncertainty.

### Reject or revise if
- Using a JPEG screenshot as ground truth; fitting shader colour to unknown tone mapping; clipping chrome or lamp highlights.
- the candidate cannot be reproduced in a fresh Blender 5.0.1 file;
- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.


## EXP-ABR4-002 — HDR highlight and reflection capture

**Category:** Reference acquisition and metrology

### Hypothesis
- Implementing hdr highlight and reflection capture with the stated physical ownership and state will outperform a generic appearance-only approximation under held-out conditions.

### Controlled setup
- target: Capture dynamic range on chrome, clearcoat, glass, lights and polished carbon.
- implementation branch: Bracket exposures; merge only after geometric alignment; preserve each source exposure; record merge operator and ghost rejection.
- baseline A: untouched source or current production asset;
- baseline B: simplest plausible generic approximation;
- candidate: v4 physically/process-linked implementation;
- fixed camera, light, colour management and deterministic seed;
- raw and denoised outputs where path tracing is used.

### Measurements
- False-colour clipping; radiance consistency across overlapping exposure regions; raw-versus-merged comparison.
- still and motion comparison;
- identity across hero/production/proxy tiers;
- performance and memory;
- source confidence and uncertainty.

### Reject or revise if
- Recovering clipped highlight shape with generative tools; treating bloom as material energy.
- the candidate cannot be reproduced in a fresh Blender 5.0.1 file;
- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.


## EXP-ABR4-003 — Cross-polarised reference capture

**Category:** Reference acquisition and metrology

### Hypothesis
- Implementing cross-polarised reference capture with the stated physical ownership and state will outperform a generic appearance-only approximation under held-out conditions.

### Controlled setup
- target: Separate much of the surface-interface reflection from body or subsurface contribution.
- implementation branch: Capture parallel and crossed states with fixed exposure geometry; calibrate polariser angles; retain unpolarised state.
- baseline A: untouched source or current production asset;
- baseline B: simplest plausible generic approximation;
- candidate: v4 physically/process-linked implementation;
- fixed camera, light, colour management and deterministic seed;
- raw and denoised outputs where path tracing is used.

### Measurements
- Parallel-minus-cross residual; exposure-normalised comparison; metallic and carbon control samples.
- still and motion comparison;
- identity across hero/production/proxy tiers;
- performance and memory;
- source confidence and uncertainty.

### Reject or revise if
- Calling the crossed image pure diffuse; using it to remove all clearcoat or metal reflection.
- the candidate cannot be reproduced in a fresh Blender 5.0.1 file;
- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.

- source confidence and uncertainty.

- Comparing unnormalised exposures; assuming metallic surfaces obey dielectric separation.

- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

## EXP-ABR4-006 — Photometric stereo for normal recovery

**Category:** Reference acquisition and metrology

### Hypothesis
- Implementing photometric stereo for normal recovery with the stated physical ownership and state will outperform a generic appearance-only approximation under held-out conditions.

### Controlled setup
- target: Recover local normals from fixed-view multi-light images, including advanced non-Lambertian methods.
- implementation branch: Capture many calibrated light directions; mask cast shadows and saturated highlights; compare classical and robust/non-Lambertian solvers.
- baseline A: untouched source or current production asset;
- baseline B: simplest plausible generic approximation;
- candidate: v4 physically/process-linked implementation;
- fixed camera, light, colour management and deterministic seed;
- raw and denoised outputs where path tracing is used.

### Measurements
- Normal angular consistency; integrability; reconstructed strip-light render.
- still and motion comparison;
- identity across hero/production/proxy tiers;
- performance and memory;
- source confidence and uncertainty.

### Reject or revise if
- Using three lights on automotive paint and trusting the result; confusing reflectance variation with shape.
- the candidate cannot be reproduced in a fresh Blender 5.0.1 file;
- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.

- source confidence and uncertainty.

- Diagnosing every stripe distortion as topology; ignoring contamination and material roughness.

- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

- Implementing visible edge radii and hemming with the stated physical ownership and state will outperform a generic appearance-only approximation under held-out conditions.

### Controlled setup
- target: Outer panel, trim, grille and lamp edges have process-dependent radii.
- implementation branch: Use measured or reference-derived radii by part class.
- baseline A: untouched source or current production asset;
- baseline B: simplest plausible generic approximation;
- candidate: v4 physically/process-linked implementation;
- fixed camera, light, colour management and deterministic seed;
- raw and denoised outputs where path tracing is used.

### Measurements
- Section views; radius ladder; highlight width.
- still and motion comparison;
- identity across hero/production/proxy tiers;
- performance and memory;
- source confidence and uncertainty.

### Reject or revise if
- Global Bevel with one width; infinitely sharp chrome trim.
- the candidate cannot be reproduced in a fresh Blender 5.0.1 file;
- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.

**Category:** Adhesives, sealants, foams and bonded assemblies

### Hypothesis
- Implementing expandable foam and acoustic filler with the stated physical ownership and state will outperform a generic appearance-only approximation under held-out conditions.

### Controlled setup
- target: Fill cavities, seal airflow and damp vibration.
- implementation branch: Use low-detail volume/mesh only where exposed; tag hidden function.
- baseline A: untouched source or current production asset;
- baseline B: simplest plausible generic approximation;
- candidate: v4 physically/process-linked implementation;
- fixed camera, light, colour management and deterministic seed;
- raw and denoised outputs where path tracing is used.

### Measurements
- Cutaway; cavity occupancy; compression.
- still and motion comparison;
- identity across hero/production/proxy tiers;
- performance and memory;
- source confidence and uncertainty.

### Reject or revise if
- Filling all hidden cavities with visible foam.
- the candidate cannot be reproduced in a fresh Blender 5.0.1 file;
- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.

- Implementing bond-line fillet and squeeze-out with the stated physical ownership and state will outperform a generic appearance-only approximation under held-out conditions.

- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

## EXP-ABR4-066 — Corrosion after repair or replacement

**Category:** Corrosion, galvanic interaction and long-term weathering

### Hypothesis
- Implementing corrosion after repair or replacement with the stated physical ownership and state will outperform a generic appearance-only approximation under held-out conditions.

### Controlled setup
- target: Poor surface preparation, seam sealing or coating overlap changes failure pattern.
- implementation branch: Blend edge, filler, primer, sealer and panel replacement IDs.
- baseline A: untouched source or current production asset;
- baseline B: simplest plausible generic approximation;
- candidate: v4 physically/process-linked implementation;
- fixed camera, light, colour management and deterministic seed;
- raw and denoised outputs where path tracing is used.

### Measurements
- Factory versus repair ageing.
- still and motion comparison;
- identity across hero/production/proxy tiers;
- performance and memory;
- source confidence and uncertainty.

### Reject or revise if
- Random rust used to imply age without repair history.
- the candidate cannot be reproduced in a fresh Blender 5.0.1 file;
- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.

- target: Paint colour and formulation affect returned intensity and detection.
- implementation branch: Sensor-emitter/receiver model or calibrated intensity shader branch.
- baseline A: untouched source or current production asset;
- baseline B: simplest plausible generic approximation;
- candidate: v4 physically/process-linked implementation;
- fixed camera, light, colour management and deterministic seed;
- raw and denoised outputs where path tracing is used.

### Measurements
- Several sensors, ranges, angles and paints.
- still and motion comparison;
- identity across hero/production/proxy tiers;
- performance and memory;
- source confidence and uncertainty.

### Reject or revise if
- One universal LiDAR material parameter.
- the candidate cannot be reproduced in a fresh Blender 5.0.1 file;
- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.

- Placement and layer ownership.
- still and motion comparison;
- identity across hero/production/proxy tiers;
- performance and memory;
- source confidence and uncertainty.

### Reject or revise if
- Publishing real personal VIN data or random impossible formats.
- the candidate cannot be reproduced in a fresh Blender 5.0.1 file;
- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.

## EXP-ABR4-133 — Polarisation state capture

**Category:** Spectral, colour, polarisation and camera-reference governance

### Hypothesis
- Implementing polarisation state capture with the stated physical ownership and state will outperform a generic appearance-only approximation under held-out conditions.

### Controlled setup
- target: Clearcoat, fibres, glass, water and metals interact with polarisation differently.
- implementation branch: Parallel/cross/unpolarised triplet and angle.
- baseline A: untouched source or current production asset;
- baseline B: simplest plausible generic approximation;
- candidate: v4 physically/process-linked implementation;
- fixed camera, light, colour management and deterministic seed;
- raw and denoised outputs where path tracing is used.

### Measurements
- Degree-of-polarisation proxy.
- still and motion comparison;
- identity across hero/production/proxy tiers;
- performance and memory;
- source confidence and uncertainty.

### Reject or revise if
- Claiming a standard RGB shader reproduces polarisation.
- the candidate cannot be reproduced in a fresh Blender 5.0.1 file;
- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.

- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.


## EXP-ABR4-156 — Free, installed and service states

**Category:** Thermal, mechanical and assembly-state systems

### Hypothesis
- Implementing free, installed and service states with the stated physical ownership and state will outperform a generic appearance-only approximation under held-out conditions.

### Controlled setup
- target: A part may look different removed, loosely fitted or fully clipped/bonded.
- implementation branch: State collection, pivots and deformation.
- baseline A: untouched source or current production asset;
- baseline B: simplest plausible generic approximation;
- candidate: v4 physically/process-linked implementation;
- fixed camera, light, colour management and deterministic seed;
- raw and denoised outputs where path tracing is used.

### Measurements
- Installed/removed/service render.
- still and motion comparison;
- identity across hero/production/proxy tiers;
- performance and memory;
- source confidence and uncertainty.

### Reject or revise if
- Using installed outer shell as complete standalone part.
- the candidate cannot be reproduced in a fresh Blender 5.0.1 file;
- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.

- target: May replace chrome with gloss black, satin black, dark PVD or anodised trim.
- implementation branch: Map each part to actual candidate stack.
- baseline A: untouched source or current production asset;
- baseline B: simplest plausible generic approximation;
- candidate: v4 physically/process-linked implementation;
- fixed camera, light, colour management and deterministic seed;
- raw and denoised outputs where path tracing is used.

### Measurements
- Grazing and damage comparison.
- still and motion comparison;
- identity across hero/production/proxy tiers;
- performance and memory;
- source confidence and uncertainty.

### Reject or revise if
- One black material assigned to entire package.
- the candidate cannot be reproduced in a fresh Blender 5.0.1 file;
- the added complexity does not survive a held-out light, view or state;
- the method destroys useful game-source data or creates unexplained dependencies.

### Decision values
- `ACCEPT`;
- `REVISE`;
- `REJECT`;
- `CONTEXT_DEPENDENT`;
- `SOURCE_INSUFFICIENT`.

- **Author/publisher:** Li et al.
- **Year/version:** 2020
- **Type:** primary research
- **URL:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2001.06659
- **Supports:** joint shape and spatially varying isotropic reflectance capture; reported controlled-setup accuracy belongs to that study, not to full-car glossy capture
